events.descriptionPlain: Looking for a fun way to start the semester and meet new people on campus? Join ChinaBlue for some summer games! events.name: Create a Bouquet with CPA
events.descriptionPlain: The College Panhellenic Association invites students to join us for a Bouquet Making Recruitment Event in collaboration with our three National Panhellenic Conference sororities on campus: Alpha Sigma Alpha, Sigma Delta Tau, and Theta Phi Alpha. This event is designed to provide students with an opportunity to learn more about Panhellenic life, meet active members from each of the three sororities, and gain insight into the values, sisterhood, and experiences that Panhellenic membership offers. In a relaxed and welcoming setting, participants will create their own personalized floral bouquets to take home. The bouquet-making activity serves as both a fun creative outlet and a way to foster conversations and connections among students and sorority members. The College Panhellenic Association (CPA) serves as the governing body for the Panhellenic sororities at Stony Brook University and is committed to promoting scholarship, leadership, service, and community among its members. This recruitment event reflects CPA’s mission of creating meaningful opportunities for students to engage with Greek Life while learning more about how to get involved on campus. All students are welcome to attend, and no prior experience with crafts or knowledge of Greek Life is required. This event is open to anyone interested in exploring Panhellenic opportunities or simply looking to enjoy a hands-on activity while meeting new people
events.descriptionHtml:
The College Panhellenic Association invites students to join us for a Bouquet Making Recruitment Event in collaboration with our three National Panhellenic Conference sororities on campus: Alpha Sigma Alpha, Sigma Delta Tau, and Theta Phi Alpha.

In a relaxed and welcoming setting, participants will create their own personalized floral bouquets to take home. The bouquet-making activity serves as both a fun creative outlet and a way to foster conversations and connections among students and sorority members.
The College Panhellenic Association (CPA) serves as the governing body for the Panhellenic sororities at Stony Brook University and is committed to promoting scholarship, leadership, service, and community among its members. events.name: MACHTail Mixer
events.descriptionPlain: As part of Welcome Back Week, we are pleased to invite you to the “MACH”-Tail Mixer at The Space, our makerspace dedicated to Manufacturing, Assembly, Collaboration, and Hands-on Innovation. This event is an opportunity to connect with students, staff/faculty, and SBU alumni while exploring the exciting projects and resources available here within the MACH 1 Innovation Hub. Location: The Space (Engineering 105) Date: August 28th, 2025 Time: 5pm - 7pm Light refreshments and “mocktails” will be served. We hope you’ll join us in celebrating the start of a new semester and in fostering connections across our community of makers, innovators, and problem-solvers.
events.descriptionHtml:
As part of Welcome Back Week, we are pleased to invite you to the “MACH”-Tail Mixer at The Space, our makerspace dedicated to Manufacturing, Assembly, Collaboration, and Hands-on Innovation.

This event is an opportunity to connect with students, staff/faculty, and SBU alumni while exploring the exciting projects and resources available here within the MACH 1 Innovation Hub.

Location: The Space (Engineering 105)
Date: August 28th, 2025
Time: 5pm - 7pm

Light refreshments and “mocktails” will be served.

We hope you’ll join us in celebrating the start of a new semester and in fostering connections across our community of makers, innovators, and problem-solvers.

events.name: VSA Field Day!
events.descriptionPlain: Get your energy UP for VSA Field Day! 🌟🎉✨💫 Grab your name tag 🏷️, stretch it out with some friends, and dive headfirst into the FUNNEST night ever! We’re talking wild potato sack races 🥔🏃💨, partner dashes 👯💥, and epic team relays.🏅🥳Plus, some egg-and-spoon battles 🥚🥄🤣 for shiny gold medals 🥇🏆🎖️! Not in a racing mood? No worries, slide on over to the chill zone 🧊🃏🌿, where you’ll find card games 🃏, hilarious icebreakers 🤔🎲, poker headbands 🏷️🤪, and silly games like “Never Have I Ever” 😂🙌 or “Would You Rather” 🧐💬. Meet new friends 🧑🤝🧑, bond with cabinet 💬👋💫, snack on tasty treats 🍪🥤, and hydrate like a champ 💧😋. Good music 🎶, cozy chats 🗣️, happy dances 💃🕺, and all the VSA sparkle you love 💖🌈✨! Bold in the field 💪🔥, laid-back in the chill lounge 🌻🛋️pick your vibe and run with it! Hug it out 🤗, share some laughs 😂, and make the memories of a LIFETIME 📸🌟! Don’t miss out! Run, play, snack, and CHILL your heart out at Field Day! 😊✨🦋🌞 Date : September 3rd 🗓️ Time :6–8PM ⏰ Location : Staller Steps📍 Cap : 50

events.name: Intro to ASA: Matcha and Meet!
events.descriptionPlain: “Intro to ASA” is an event that serves to promote Asian American culture in Stony Brook by creating a familial atmosphere. ASA’s dedication to introduce and bring awareness to the Asian community is manifested through the progress of past events. Not only will the event display the diversity of the community, but it also showcases the various personalities of ASA’s members. Through presentations and games, Intro to ASA allows participants to get to know the members on a deeper level, segueing into the opportunity to sign up for an interview for a cabinet position.

events.name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
events.descriptionPlain: Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

events.name: Black and Latinx NEW STUDENT Welcome
events.descriptionPlain: Black and Latinx New Student Welcome | Wednesday, September 3, 2025 | 7:30 – 9:30 PM | Stony Brook Union Ballroom Congratulations and welcome to the Seawolf family! Join us for an evening of fun, friend-making, and community. This event is aimed at connecting new students to one another and to upperclass students from our Black and Latinx organizations. Enjoy a delicious Chicken, Waffles, and Ice Cream Bar while mingling with other new students and hearing from student leaders as they share what they wish they had known when they first started at SBU and the impact cultural organizations can make on your success at Stony Brook. Plus! Don’t miss out on giveaways and raffle prizes, including: Assorted Games Basket Seawolves Pride Merch & Swag College Candy & Snacks Basket Whether you’re here to meet new people, find your community, or just enjoy great food and vibes, you belong here. This event is FREE and open to all students. This event is sponsored by the Office of Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE), in collaboration with the Black Student Union (BSU), the Latin American Student Organization (LASO), and the Black and Latino Initiative for Student Success (BLISS). Be the first at our first official cross-collab event of the year.
events.descriptionHtml:
Black and Latinx New Student Welcome | Wednesday, September 3, 2025 | 7:30 – 9:30 PM | Stony Brook Union Ballroom

Congratulations and welcome to the Seawolf family!

Join us for an evening of fun, friend-making, and community.  This event is aimed at connecting new students to one another and to upperclass students from our Black and Latinx organizations. 

Enjoy a delicious Chicken, Waffles, and Ice Cream Bar while mingling with other new students and hearing from student leaders as they share what they wish they had known when they first started at SBU and the impact cultural organizations can make on your success at Stony Brook.

Plus! Don’t miss out on giveaways and raffle prizes, including:

  • Assorted Games Basket 
  • Seawolves Pride Merch & Swag
  • College Candy & Snacks Basket

Whether you’re here to meet new people, find your community, or just enjoy great food and vibes, you belong here. 
This event is FREE and open to all students.

This event is sponsored by the Office of Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE), in collaboration with the Black Student Union (BSU), the Latin American Student Organization (LASO), and the Black and Latino Initiative for Student Success (BLISS).  

Be the first at our first official cross-collab event of the year.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: Painted Playback
events.descriptionPlain: 🎨 Painted Playback 🎶 Join us for a fun and creative night where music meets art! At Painted Playback , you’ll get to decorate your very own CD with paints, markers, and stickers—turning a simple disc into a one-of-a-kind piece of art. Whether you go for bold colors, cool patterns, or fun sticker vibes, it’s all about making it your own. ✨ All supplies are provided, just bring your creativity! 🎶 Hang out, paint, and enjoy the throwback feel of making your own “album.”

events.name: FCC Peanut Butter Noodle Night
events.descriptionPlain: Get ready for one of the Fujianese Culture Club's signature traditions: PB Noodle Night! Centered around the classic Fujianese dish, ban mian (peanut butter noodles), this event brings together the beautiful culture surrounding this dish. Come enjoy a bowl of rich, flavorful noodles while diving into interactive games and activities inspired by peanut butter noodles. Whether you’re here to eat, play, or learn, PB Noodle Night is the perfect chance to experience the taste of Fujianese culture and connect with FCC.

events.name: Meet The Statesman: School Newspaper Mixer
events.descriptionPlain: Are you curious about how stories make it from the hallways to the headlines? Interested in writing, photography, design or just love a good story? Come join us at the Meet The Statesman: School Newspaper Mixer — an open event for students to connect with the team behind the award-winning publication! This is your chance to: Meet editors, writers and photographers Learn how to get involved in upcoming print issues Pitch ideas or ask questions about the publication process Discover ways to contribute (even if writing isn’t your thing!) Free Catering Provided! Whether you’re a seasoned journalist or just newspaper-curious, everyone is welcome. Bring your appetite and your ideas — see you there!
events.descriptionHtml:
Are you curious about how stories make it from the hallways to the headlines? Interested in writing, photography, design or just love a good story? Come join us at the Meet The Statesman: School Newspaper Mixer — an open event for students to connect with the team behind the award-winning publication!

This is your chance to:

  • Meet editors, writers and photographers

  • Learn how to get involved in upcoming print issues

  • Pitch ideas or ask questions about the publication process

  • Discover ways to contribute (even if writing isn’t your thing!)

Free Catering Provided!

Whether you’re a seasoned journalist or just newspaper-curious, everyone is welcome.

Bring your appetite and your ideas — see you there!

events.name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
events.descriptionPlain: Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

Are you interested in business, technology, engineering, communications, journalism, healthcare, or the legal field?

If you answered yes, then you do not want to miss this event!

Students will have an opportunity to learn about various paid internship opportunities at top companies across the nation from the following organizations:

  • INROADS
  • HACU
  • Emma Bowen Foundation
  • T. Howard Foundation
  • MLT

Agenda:

  • Welcome
  • Panel Introductions & Discussion
  • 2 rounds of breakout sessions to learn more about the application process

LUNCH IS PROVIDED!

events.name: Dragon Boat Race Festival 2025
events.descriptionPlain: Paddlers wanted! Stony Brook University & Stony Brook Medicine employees and students are invited to participate in the Annual Port Jefferson Dragon Boat Race Festival on Saturday, September 13, 2025. This event is hosted by the Port Jefferson Chamber of Commerce and Stony Brook University is happy to be invited the Festival for the last 11 years. Complete this form by EOD, September 5, to join our teams . Team Sign Up FORM Important Information, Requirements & Dates: No paddling/rowing experience needed! Enjoy a day full of fun, competition, and recreation. Boat teams from all over Long Island and NYC will race. Breakfast and lunch will be provided on Race Day. Paddlers will receive an SBU Dragon Boat Race team shirt. All Seawolves can also join us for the day to cheer us on and enjoy the events Team Info Meeting : Friday, September 12 from 1:00 - 2:30 pm - UNITI Cultural Center (SB Union 113) Open to all; Required for undergraduate students Team Practice : Friday, September 12 at 5:00 - 6:00 pm - Port Jefferson Harbor Front Park Marina Race Day : Saturday, September 13 - Arrive at 8:45 am - PJ Harbor Front Park The first race starts at 9:00 am; the Festival ends around 5:00 pm with the Awards & Medals Ceremony Participants must be able to swim (life jackets provided) Participants should have transportation; public transportation is available from SBU to PJ Village Stony Brook's participation in the Dragon Boat Race is sponsored by the Offices of the President, University and Hospital Community Relations, Diversity, Intercultural, and Community Engagement (DICE), and the Office of the Vice President for Student Affairs. *For more information about the SB Seawolves Boat: Contact Judy Jaquez at judy.jaquez@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-9912. *For more information about SB Medicine Boat: Contact Kristin Cuomo at kristin.cuomo@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-6451. Map/Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/ra841pT2U6QjM9iY7
events.descriptionHtml:
Paddlers wanted! 

Stony Brook University & Stony Brook Medicine employees and students are invited to participate in the Annual Port Jefferson Dragon Boat Race Festival on Saturday, September 13, 2025. This event is hosted by the Port Jefferson Chamber of Commerce and Stony Brook University is happy to be invited the Festival for the last 11 years.

Complete this form by EOD, September 5, to join our teams. Team Sign Up FORM

Important Information, Requirements & Dates:
No paddling/rowing experience needed!  Enjoy a day full of fun, competition, and recreation. Boat teams from all over Long Island and NYC will race. 
Breakfast and lunch will be provided on Race Day. Paddlers will receive an SBU Dragon Boat Race team shirt. 
All Seawolves can also join us for the day to cheer us on and enjoy the events 

  • Team Info Meeting: Friday, September 12 from 1:00 - 2:30 pm - UNITI Cultural Center (SB Union 113) Open to all; Required for undergraduate students
  • Team Practice: Friday, September 12 at 5:00 - 6:00 pm - Port Jefferson Harbor Front Park Marina
  • Race Day: Saturday, September 13 - Arrive at 8:45 am  - PJ Harbor Front Park
    The first race starts at 9:00 am; the Festival ends around 5:00 pm with the Awards & Medals Ceremony
  • Participants must be able to swim (life jackets provided)
  • Participants should have transportation; public transportation is available from SBU to PJ Village

Stony Brook's participation in the Dragon Boat Race is sponsored by the Offices of the President, University and Hospital Community Relations, Diversity, Intercultural, and Community Engagement (DICE), and the Office of the Vice President for Student Affairs.

*For more information about the SB Seawolves Boat: Contact Judy Jaquez at judy.jaquez@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-9912.

*For more information about SB Medicine Boat: Contact Kristin Cuomo at kristin.cuomo@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-6451.

events.name: Book Launch: Giulia Riccò, The Italian Colony of São Paulo: Race, Class, and Cultural Capital in Brazil
events.descriptionPlain: Dr. Giulia Riccò will present her new book, The Italian Colony of São Paulo: Race, Class, and Cultural Capital in Brazil , winner of the 2024 Aldo and Jeanne Scaglione Publication Award, MLA. This book argues that Italians first became racialized as white in São Paulo, Brazil, at the turn of the twentieth century. The book is available Open Access at Fordham Commons . Dr. Riccò will be introduced by D’Amato Chair Dr. Loredana Polezzi, and following the presentation, Dr. Mary Jane Dempsey (LCS) and Simone Brione (English) will offer a response to the book. Dr. Giulia Riccò is Assistant Professor of Romance Languages and Literatures at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. As a scholar of modern Italian and Brazilian literature who studies class, migration, and race, Dr. Riccò’s research addresses how the concept of Italian identity evolved beyond the strict geographical confines of the Italian nation-state.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: Be Well Fest
events.descriptionPlain: Get Ready for the Third Annual Be Well Fest! Join us in celebrating the return of the Be Well Fest. The perfect way to kick off the semester at our Student Health and Counseling Center! Enjoy outdoor activities, grab exciting giveaways, savor freshly popped popcorn, and cool off with free ice cream. Step inside to explore our health and counseling spaces, where you can meet the friendly staff from Student Health Services (SHS) and Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS). Everyone who signs up for a tour will receive limited vouchers for on-campus Dunkin’ or Starbucks. A talented DJ will set the mood with your favorite tunes, and outdoor games like cornhole will be available, so bring your game face! As a special creative touch, you can also participate in tie-dyeing activities led by the Center for Prevention and Outreach (CPO) team. Stay healthy this semester by getting your flu vaccine at the first flu vaccine pod of the fall. The flu vaccine is one of the most effective ways to protect yourself and contribute to a healthier campus community. The first 200 students to complete a building tour will receive a free voucher for Starbucks or Dunkin’ Donuts! Tours will be held every 20 minutes, starting at 12:00 PM, with the final tour at 2:40 PM. Mark your calendars for this exciting day of community, connection, and celebration. We can't wait to see you there!

events.name: National Voter Registration Day
events.descriptionPlain: We’re excited to once again be part of the national effort to strengthen our democracy by registering voters on this year’s National Voter Registration Day , which will take place on September 17th, 2025! First celebrated in 2012, National Voter Registration Day is a nonpartisan holiday that’s helped more than 5 million people register to vote and ensure they are ready to hit the polls in their next election. This year's National Voter Registration Day will take place on Wednesday, September 18th from 12:00PM- 2:00PM in the SAC Plaza! Engage with our many student organizations and departmental partners, grab some prizes and giveaways, and learn more about how you can be prepared to participate in the upcoming election.

  • Hear about our upcoming events, tabling, and volunteer opportunities 👏

  • Connect with other passionate students making a difference 🤗

Plus: Free food will be provided! 🍽️

Together, we can grow the registry and bring hope to patients everywhere! 💙💛

events.name: Wolfieland 2025
events.descriptionPlain: Join us on September 20th from 4 to 11 PM in the SAC Parking Lots 12 & 13 ! Wolfieland is the Undergraduate Student Government's annual carnival, hosting up to 6,000+ people! Grab your cowboy boots and pop out for some exhilarating rides, inflatables, games, food, giveaways, prizes, music, dancing, and more! To find out more about what's at Wolfieland, check out our brochure at this link . Additionally, from 6-9 pm every hour on the hour, attendees will be randomly selected to win door prizes sponsored by the Auxiliary Services Association (mini fridge, guitar, bicycle, and iPad Pro), and feature performances from PUSO Modern, KBS, Jubilé Latino, and L'Afrique! This event is free for all undergraduate students and is funded by your Student Activity Fee . Free admission wristbands will also be available to the first 750 graduate students who complete the waiver and pick up a bracelet, courtesy of the Graduate Student Organization. Wristbands are required for entry and are only given to students who complete the waiver . Wristbands are required for mechanical rides and to play games/win prizes! Non-students may roam the event, buy food, etc, but will be unable to participate in mechanical rides or play games/win prizes. Students with wristbands are eligible to receive a free limited-edition Wolfieland shirt while supplies last at our wristband pick-up stations beginning Wednesday, 9/17 (see below for more details). We encourage everyone to pick up their wristband before Saturday, September 20th, to avoid the extreme rush we anticipate at the ticket office the day of the event. For both early and regular pick-up, please bring your SBU ID Card. See hours below: Wednesday, September 17th 9 am to 5 pm, Union L03-01 (Basement Rehearsal Room) Thursday, September 18th 9 am to 5 pm, Behind the Student Union (Facing Student Health Service Building. The line will form between the Union and East Side Dining.) Friday, September 19th 9 am to 5 pm, Behind the Student Union (Facing Student Health Service Building. The line will form between the Union and East Side Dining.) 7:30 pm to 7:45 pm, Staller Lobby before Superman Viewing Saturday, September 20th 9 am to 4 pm, SAC Ticket Office 4 pm to 11 pm, at Wolfieland at our two registration tents, one closest to the Student Activities Center, and the other, closest to West Side Dining Please note : Wolfieland is a great time , but it's a long time & a highly attended event with large crowds . Prepare accordingly! Bring a charged phone, water, sunscreen, bug spray, & patience as there will be long lines. For any accessibility needs/accommodations, please contact usg_studentlife@ stonybrook.edu .
events.descriptionHtml:
Join us on September 20th from 4 to 11 PM in the SAC Parking Lots 12 & 13! Wolfieland is the Undergraduate Student Government's annual carnival, hosting up to 6,000+ people! Grab your cowboy boots and pop out for some exhilarating rides, inflatables, games, food, giveaways, prizes, music, dancing, and more! To find out more about what's at Wolfieland, check out our brochure at this link. Additionally, from 6-9 pm every hour on the hour, attendees will be randomly selected to win door prizes sponsored by the Auxiliary Services Association (mini fridge, guitar, bicycle, and iPad Pro), and feature performances from PUSO Modern, KBS, Jubilé Latino, and L'Afrique!

 

This event is free for all undergraduate students and is funded by your Student Activity Fee. Free admission wristbands will also be available to the first 750 graduate students who complete the waiver and pick up a bracelet, courtesy of the Graduate Student Organization.

 

Wristbands are required for entry and are only given to students who complete the waiver. Wristbands are required for mechanical rides and to play games/win prizes! Non-students may roam the event, buy food, etc, but will be unable to participate in mechanical rides or play games/win prizes. 

 

Students with wristbands are eligible to receive a free limited-edition Wolfieland shirt while supplies last at our wristband pick-up stations beginning Wednesday, 9/17 (see below for more details). We encourage everyone to pick up their wristband before Saturday, September 20th, to avoid the extreme rush we anticipate at the ticket office the day of the event. For both early and regular pick-up, please bring your SBU ID Card. See hours below:

 

Wednesday, September 17th

9 am to 5 pm, Union L03-01 (Basement Rehearsal Room)

 

Thursday, September 18th

9 am to 5 pm, Behind the Student Union (Facing Student Health Service Building. The line will form between the Union and East Side Dining.)

 

Friday, September 19th

9 am to 5 pm, Behind the Student Union (Facing Student Health Service Building. The line will form between the Union and East Side Dining.)
7:30 pm to 7:45 pm, Staller Lobby before Superman Viewing

 

Saturday, September 20th

9 am to 4 pm, SAC Ticket Office
4 pm to 11 pm, at Wolfieland at our two registration tents, one closest to the Student Activities Center, and the other, closest to West Side Dining

 

Please note: Wolfieland is a great time, but it's a long time & a highly attended event with large crowds. Prepare accordingly! Bring a charged phone, water, sunscreen, bug spray, & patience as there will be long lines.

 

For any accessibility needs/accommodations, please contact usg_studentlife@stonybrook.edu.

events.name: Garba Night 2025
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for the second annual Garba Night. It is going to be an unforgettable celebration that you will not want to miss! All are welcome to partake in a vibrant evening filled with delicious food, lively dancing, and lots of fun. Enjoy captivating performances and immerse yourself in the spirit of Navratri as we come together as a community. This is a wonderful opportunity to connect with Chapin staff, IGSA, and Center for Indian Studies and to learn more about our rich traditions. Whether you're a seasoned dancer or new to the festivities, there's something for everyone. Don't miss out on this chance to celebrate with us—bring your friends for a night of joy and unity! We can't wait to see you there!

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.
events.descriptionHtml:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: STEM Carnival
events.descriptionPlain: The 2025 Stony Brook iGEM Team is excited to host a STEM Carnival in collaboration with campus organizations, including NMDP, APSA, ACS, Medlife, BMES, Bioethics, and many more. This event will feature engaging STEM-themed games, interactive activities, and exciting opportunities to connect with student clubs that you can get involved with on campus. You’ll also learn about research opportunities, outreach initiatives, and ways to build community in science and medicine at Stony Brook. Plus, attendees will have the chance to win amazing prizes such as a heated blanket, Japanese snack and candy box, book nook kit, matcha kit, ice maker machine, Egg Jellycat, electric ramen cooker, etc. Don’t miss this fun and rewarding way to explore STEM while discovering new ways to get involved!

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: GradConnect Series
events.descriptionPlain: We are excited to invite graduate students to the first-ever GradConnect Series , organized by the Graduate School. This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community. For the first GradConnect Series event, we are delighted to welcome the Graduate School leadership team, who will be there to interact with students, including; Celia Marshik , Dean of the Graduate School and Vice Provost for Graduate Education David A Rubenstein , Associate Dean for Academic and Student Affairs Elizabeth Boon , Associate Dean of Strategic Initiatives Christine Greenberg , Director of Student Services Light refreshments will be provided. Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events (September-December) in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December! Please stay tuned for more details and dates. We hope you’ll join us for this exciting kickoff and take advantage of the chance to connect with both the Graduate School team and fellow graduate students.
events.descriptionHtml:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the first-ever GradConnect Series, organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the first GradConnect Series event, we are delighted to welcome the Graduate School leadership team, who will be there to interact with students, including;

 

Celia Marshik, Dean of the Graduate School and Vice Provost for Graduate Education

David A Rubenstein, Associate Dean for Academic and Student Affairs

Elizabeth Boon, Associate Dean of Strategic Initiatives

Christine Greenberg, Director of Student Services

 

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events (September-December) in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  Please stay tuned for more details and dates.

 

We hope you’ll join us for this exciting kickoff and take advantage of the chance to connect with both the Graduate School team and fellow graduate students.

events.name: Heat & Heat: Fire Safety and Prevention
events.descriptionPlain: Fire prevention at Stony Brook University is a year-round effort by Fire and Emergency Services. During the month of September, we conduct numerous fire safety lectures, fire extinguisher training, and live-burn demonstrations in each of the residential communities and host an annual fire and safety expo on the academic mall. Join us and the first 300 participants will get a voucher for food and refreshment for the event.

events.name: NRHH's Sustainability Week
events.descriptionPlain: Get ready for a week full of green vibes, creative reuse, and community impact! NRHH is celebrating Sustainability Week with a lineup of events that bring environmental action to life — from peaceful nature walks and hands-on campus cleanups to DIY projects that turn trash into treasure. Whether you're folding boats from recycled paper, digging into a little gardening, or just taking in the fresh air by Roth Pond, there's something for everyone! Lasting Monday 9/29 to Friday 10/3. Monday, 9/29 – Nature Walk Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM Meet outside Javits by the bike station. We'll walk around Javits, then head toward Roth Pond for a walk around the water. The walk will end either back at the bike station or near the pond. Tuesday, 9/30 – Campus Clean-Up Time: 1:30 PM – 3:00 PM Follow the same route as Monday’s walk, but this time we’ll focus on picking up trash along the way. Bring gloves if you have them! Wednesday, 10/1 – Plastic Bottle DIY & Gardening Time: 5:00 PM – 6:00 PM Join us outside Mendy at the red tables between Gray and Ammann for a fun DIY session using plastic bottles to create simple gardening containers. Thursday, 10/2 – Origami Night: Folding with Recycled Paper Time: 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM Come join us for a relaxing origami night where we’ll be folding paper boats and other designs using recycled paper. As part of our sustainability efforts, this event highlights the importance of reusing paper in creative ways. No experience needed — just drop in, fold, and have fun while being eco-conscious! Friday, 10/3 – Nature Walk: Part 2 Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM If you missed the first one — or just want another chance to unwind outdoors — join us for Nature Walk: Part 2. We'll meet outside Javits by the bike station, walk through the same route around Javits, and head back toward Roth Pond. Along the way, we’ll take time to enjoy the scenery, chat, and reflect on the week. Feel free to bring a friend, a reusable water bottle, or just yourself!
events.descriptionHtml:
Get ready for a week full of green vibes, creative reuse, and community impact! NRHH is celebrating Sustainability Week with a lineup of events that bring environmental action to life — from peaceful nature walks and hands-on campus cleanups to DIY projects that turn trash into treasure. Whether you're folding boats from recycled paper, digging into a little gardening, or just taking in the fresh air by Roth Pond, there's something for everyone! 

Lasting Monday 9/29 to Friday 10/3.

 

Monday, 9/29 – Nature Walk

Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM

Meet outside Javits by the bike station. We'll walk around Javits, then head toward Roth Pond for a walk around the water. The walk will end either back at the bike station or near the pond.

 

Tuesday, 9/30 – Campus Clean-Up

Time: 1:30 PM – 3:00 PM

Follow the same route as Monday’s walk, but this time we’ll focus on picking up trash along the way. Bring gloves if you have them!

 

Wednesday, 10/1 – Plastic Bottle DIY & Gardening

Time: 5:00 PM – 6:00 PM

Join us outside Mendy at the red tables between Gray and Ammann for a fun DIY session using plastic bottles to create simple gardening containers.

 

Thursday, 10/2 – Origami Night: Folding with Recycled Paper

Time: 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM

Come join us for a relaxing origami night where we’ll be folding paper boats and other designs using recycled paper. As part of our sustainability efforts, this event highlights the importance of reusing paper in creative ways. No experience needed — just drop in, fold, and have fun while being eco-conscious!

 

Friday, 10/3 – Nature Walk: Part 2

Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM

If you missed the first one — or just want another chance to unwind outdoors — join us for Nature Walk: Part 2. We'll meet outside Javits by the bike station, walk through the same route around Javits, and head back toward Roth Pond. Along the way, we’ll take time to enjoy the scenery, chat, and reflect on the week. Feel free to bring a friend, a reusable water bottle, or just yourself!

events.name: Monkeys: The Gardeners of the Forest (Ecology & Evolution Society Club GBM)
events.descriptionPlain: Thinking about a career in Ecology or Evolution? Come join us this Tuesday, Sept 30 in Life Science Rm 650 for an exciting Eco & Evo Club meeting! We're hosting special guest Ariek Norford who will present: “Monkeys: The Gardeners of the Forest” - Get a wild look at how both common and threatened monkey species play a key role in seed dispersal and forest health. We’ll also be talking about career paths in the Ecology & Evolution field and how you can start getting involved as a student. Free Food , snacks, drinks, and chill vibes guaranteed. Bring a friend and your curiosity — we’d love to see you there!

events.name: Pre-Health APAMSA: Health Insurance 101
events.descriptionPlain: Health insurance may feel complicated due to the various plans and benefits that are covered, but it plays a crucial role in ensuring our access to health services. Walk away with practical tips that can help you (and your family) navigate healthcare with confidence.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza.

The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: 2025 Hispanic Heritage Month Opening Celebration
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for Stony Brook University’s 36th Annual Hispanic Heritage Month Celebration ! The event kicks off with the Latin American Flags Procession led by Wolfie and the Spirit of Stony Brook Marching Band , followed by cultural performances and interactive activity tables hosted by Latino student clubs. Highlights include a keynote address by Deputy Speaker of the New York State Assembly Phil Ramos and the official Proclamation presented by President Andrea Goldsmith . Celebrate tradition, community, and pride with us! Co-sponsored by the Hispanic Heritage Month Committee, Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE), Diversity, Inclusion and Intercultural Initiatives (DI3), and Universitarios Unidos Latinoamericanos.

events.name: Hidden Treasures x Fashion Club: Closet Remix: Swap, Sell, Style
events.descriptionPlain: Join Hidden Treasures and the Fashion Club for an engaging event that combines fashion, sustainability, and practical skills! Closet Remix offers students the opportunity to refresh their wardrobes while learning how to resell clothing through platforms such as Depop. ✨The event will include a workshop on creating a successful online shop— from photographing and listing items to pricing and marketing strategies — as well as a clothing swap where participants can bring gently used pieces to exchange for new-to-you finds.✨ Whether you are looking to explore resale platforms, give your clothing a second life, or discover unique styles from your peers, this event provides a space to learn, connect, and elevate your personal style responsibly. 🌱

events.name: Wellness Fair
events.descriptionPlain: Join the Skincare and Wellness Club for our third annual Wellness Fair ! This year's theme is Beauty and the BS , and with it, the Wellness Fair will dive into common myths about wellness, highlight sustainable and accessible self-care practices, and empower attendees to make informed choices for their health and the planet. By working with 13 collaborating clubs and organizations, we're bringing you a campus-wide celebration of health & community. Here's what you can expect to find at the Wellness Fair: Plenty of free swag, including raffle prizes DIY Skincare and other creative crafts Snacks & refreshments Wellness tips and resources And a newfound sense of clarity and confidence in your self-care routines! Whether you’re looking for stress relief, sustainable skincare ideas, or simply a supportive community, there’s something here for everyone. We hope to see you there! Please reach out to sbu.skincare@gmail.com with any accessibility concerns.

events.name: Inspiring the Future: Latinos in Health Care | Faculty Panel and Meet & Greet
events.descriptionPlain: Inspiring the Future: Latinos in Health Care Faculty Panel and Meet & Greet Wednesday, October 1st, 2025 | 6:00 PM –8:00 PM | UNITI Cultural Center - SB Union 113 Join us for a dynamic Health Professions Panel where SBU’s own Latino faculty from Stony Brook Medicine and the Health Sciences will share their journeys. This event is an opportunity to hear first-hand from accomplished professionals about the challenges and rewards of working in health fields, the importance of representation, and strategies for success. Come meet the panel, ask your questions in a small, intimate setting to get you closer to achieving your dreams. Panelists: Carley Gomes, MD – Neonatology Gloria Viboud, PhD, SM (ASCP) – Clinical Laboratory Sciences Giselle Gerardi, PhD, RN, RNC-OB, C-EFM – School of Nursing Maria E. Torres, PhD, MA – Associate Dean for Academic Affairs, School of Social Welfare Rosalia Casanova, OTD, OTR/L – Occupational Therapy This event is free and open to all students. Seating is limited. Refreshments will be served Sponsored by Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement; Student Life at the Health Sciences Center; and the School of Health Professions.

events.descriptionPlain: Join RAs Alvin, Bella, and Zarif on learning the environmental impact that lantern flies have. We will understand why their invasion is such a big problem, as well as why it's so important to squash them when you see them. Through a fun kahoot, winners will win a Squishmallow, collectible Micromallow , or a handmade crochet bug keychain ! Free snacks provided on a first come first serve basis. Wednesday October 1st at 8pm in the Tubman MPR. For any accessibility concerns, please contact isabella.salgado@stonybrook.edu

events.name: Canvas of cultures: Hispanic heritage fest
events.descriptionPlain: 📅 Date: Tuesday, October 1st 🕣 Time: 8:30 PM – 10:00 PM 📍 Location: Roth Café 122 Come celebrate Hispanic Heritage Fest with us! Enjoy tasty empanadas, cultural snacks, and refreshing traditional drinks while connecting with the Latinos Business Association, Dominican Student Alliance, Ecuadorian Student Organization, and Mexican Student Organization. Each club will share more about who they are, what they do, and how you can get involved. Plus, learn about upcoming Cardozo Hall Council events and opportunities to engage with your campus community. It’s a night of food, culture, and connection you won’t want to miss!

That's not all- we'll be getting in the fall spirit with pumpkin spiced cold brew and apple cider donut holes (while supplies last). You won't want to miss it ;)

events.name: Pizza' The Pie
events.descriptionPlain: FREE PIZZA!!! FREE PIZZAAAA. The first 40 minutes will be spent playing a trivia game modeled after Trivial Pursuit Live!, with rounds including Quickstarter, Grab Bag, Switchagories, Close Call, and Rapid Fire. Questions will cover a range of STEM topics such as biology, chemistry, physics, technology, and biomedical engineering. Participants earn wedges by answering questions correctly, and each wedge corresponds to slices of pizza, which will be served during the final 20 minutes of the event based on how well the team performs. The winning team will win a set of prizes as well!

events.name: Tech & Engineering Internship Application Workshop
events.descriptionPlain: In this event, students will learn tips and tricks for applying to tech and engineering internships for this upcoming summer. Students will learn about resume writing techniques, networking, ways to find opportunities, interviewing tips, and on-campus resources available to them, such as the Career Center! This event will focus exclusively on the competitive application process for tech and engineering internships. Internship search strategies will also be shared. Light refreshments will be provided! About the organizers: RA Samuel Perrottet is a senior in the Computer Science major. He has completed one summer internship at Betterment, a wealth management firm, where he will continue working after graduation this spring. He has also worked on campus as an assistant at the SeaWulf supercomputer cluster. RA Joseph Litjens is a senior in the Mechanical Engineering major. He has completed a summer internship at ASML, a semiconductor manufacturing equipment company, as well as an internship at Brookhaven National Laboratory, a national scientific research hub just a short drive away from campus. For comments, questions, or concerns, please reach out to joseph.litjens@stonybrook.edu or samuel.perrottet@stonybrook.edu
events.descriptionHtml:

This event will focus exclusively on the competitive application process for tech and engineering internships. Internship search strategies will also be shared. Light refreshments will be provided!

About the organizers:

RA Samuel Perrottet is a senior in the Computer Science major. He has completed one summer internship at Betterment, a wealth management firm, where he will continue working after graduation this spring. He has also worked on campus as an assistant at the SeaWulf supercomputer cluster.

RA Joseph Litjens is a senior in the Mechanical Engineering major. He has completed a summer internship at ASML, a semiconductor manufacturing equipment company, as well as an internship at Brookhaven National Laboratory, a national scientific research hub just a short drive away from campus.

For comments, questions, or concerns, please reach out to joseph.litjens@stonybrook.edu or samuel.perrottet@stonybrook.edu

events.name: Students with Children PLAYDATE: Fall Into Fun!
events.descriptionPlain: The Office of Student Life will be hosting a Student-with-Children Playdate on Saturday, October 4th from 10am-12pm in the Student Activities Center Ballroom for all Seawolves with children to meet up and socialize. Meet up with fellow Seawolves with children for some fall-themed fun, including crafts, family bonding, brunch refreshments, and Stony Brook swag raffles! With Halloween just a few weeks away -- COSTUMES ARE WELCOME !! Children will also be able to customize 'trick or treat' goodie bags. RSVP to let us know you're planning to attend! Reach out to student_community_development@stonybrook.edu with any questions! At this time this event is only open to Stony Brook Students who have children (parents/guardians/caregiver). Minors must be accompanied by adults at ALL times. Stony Brook University is NOT responsible for care, custody, or supervision of minors.

events.name: Inclusion Is So Fetch!
events.descriptionPlain: On October 3rd, he asked me what day it was… but on October 4th, you can join RA Tanim, RA Karina, and RA Dilsad to watch Mean Girls (2004) in the Tubman Multipurpose Room and discuss what it means to be inclusive in a world full of cliques and stereotypes. Enjoy an iconic movie while snacking on provided popcorn and chocolate! Let’s learn about how we can make our community one where everyone has a seat at the table. Come with an open mind (and in your most comfy pajamas)… because exclusion? She doesn’t even go here. We hope you join us because you CAN sit with us! 😌❤️ For accessibility concerns, please contact RA Tanim: tanim.rahman@stonybrook.edu
events.descriptionHtml:
On October 3rd, he asked me what day it was… but on October 4th, you can join RA Tanim, RA Karina, and RA Dilsad to watch Mean Girls (2004) in the Tubman Multipurpose Room and discuss what it means to be inclusive in a world full of cliques and stereotypes. Enjoy an iconic movie while snacking on provided popcorn and chocolate! Let’s learn about how we can make our community one where everyone has a seat at the table. 

 

Come with an open mind (and in your most comfy pajamas)… because exclusion? She doesn’t even go here.

We hope you join us because you CAN sit with us! 😌❤️


For accessibility concerns, please contact RA Tanim: tanim.rahman@stonybrook.edu

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: Take to: Keith Haring Coming Out Paint Day
events.descriptionPlain: Join RA Maanav as we head over to the Keith Haring Coming Out Paint Day on Tuesday, October 8th! This event celebrates National Coming Out Day through art, reflection, and community. You will have the chance to: 🎨 Paint Keith Haring–inspired designs on tote bags and canvases ✍️ Write a coming out letter to themselves or others 🖌️ Color in iconic artwork inspired by Keith Haring This is a great opportunity to honor the legacy of Keith Haring as a queer artist and activist , celebrate the courage of LGBTQ+ individuals who live openly, and create space for allies to learn and show support. Come join us for art, community, and conversation—you don’t need any art experience, just bring yourself! We will meet at the Chapin MPR at 11:30 AM and take the bus to SAC!
events.descriptionHtml:
Join RA Maanav as we head over to the Keith Haring Coming Out Paint Day on Tuesday, October 8th! This event celebrates National Coming Out Day through art, reflection, and community.

You will have the chance to:
🎨 Paint Keith Haring–inspired designs on tote bags and canvases
✍️ Write a coming out letter to themselves or others
🖌️ Color in iconic artwork inspired by Keith Haring

This is a great opportunity to honor the legacy of Keith Haring as a queer artist and activist, celebrate the courage of LGBTQ+ individuals who live openly, and create space for allies to learn and show support.

Come join us for art, community, and conversation—you don’t need any art experience, just bring yourself!

We will meet at the Chapin MPR at 11:30 AM and take the bus to SAC!

events.descriptionPlain: National Voter Education Week is a nonpartisan initiative aimed at enhancing voter engagement through collaboration with colleges, universities, corporations, and election officials. The week focuses on all aspects of voting, including voter registration and turnout. Students will be educated on various topics, such as how to complete voter registration forms, request absentee ballots, and more. Join us on Wednesday to make a plan to vote in the 2025 election. * Time and Location are TBD*

events.name: Community Dialogue: LGBTQ Rights and Local Government
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for an open and welcoming conversation exploring the intersections between LGBTQ+ identity and local government. This dialogue is meant to empower, encourage, and mobilize LGBTQ+ students and allies by creating a space to share stories, ask questions, and build community. Together, we’ll talk about the fears and challenges LGBTQ+ people may be facing today, and how those challenges can be met with strength, solidarity, and civic engagement. We’ll also highlight the power of local government: the policies and decisions that directly shape our daily lives, the opportunities for involvement, and the importance of lifting LGBTQ+ voices in our own neighborhoods.
events.descriptionHtml:
Join us for an open and welcoming conversation exploring the intersections between LGBTQ+ identity and local government. This dialogue is meant to empower, encourage, and mobilize LGBTQ+ students and allies by creating a space to share stories, ask questions, and build community.

events.name: A Cold War Era Murder Mystery
events.descriptionPlain: You've been invited to attend a diplomatic meeting. Tensions are high with regional conflicts already putting millions of lives at stake, and the thought of mutually-assured destruction is looming over your heads. However, things take a sharp turn when one of the diplomats is murdered. Can your table find out who murdered them and why before hysteria takes over? Trust nobody, not even yourselves. In this Cold War murder mystery, it is on a first-come, first-served basis. There will be free food and drinks. The team that figures out the motive and who killed our diplomat will win a prize!

events.descriptionPlain: 🚀 Internship Season is HERE! 🚀 Looking for a summer job, internship, or research opportunity? The time to apply is NOW —not next month, not next semester, but right now. Still stuck scrolling on LinkedIn? 👀 That place is way too crowded . Come learn how to: ✨ Find hidden opportunities on different platforms ✨ Apply to dozens of jobs faster with auto-fill tools (save your sanity!) ✨ Explore options for Engineering , Business , AND Pre-Med students (we’ve got clinical internships + shadowing too!) events.name: National Voter Education Week: Understand the Process
events.descriptionPlain: National Voter Education Week is a nonpartisan initiative aimed at enhancing voter engagement through collaboration with colleges, universities, corporations, and election officials. The week focuses on all aspects of voting, including voter registration and turnout. Students will be educated on various topics, such as how to complete voter registration forms, request absentee ballots, and more. Join us at our Thursday event to learn more about the election process. We will have fun games themed towards the election and its processes! *Time and Location are TBD*

events.name: PAL Varun & iGEM Present: Entering Research Panel
events.descriptionPlain: Curious about how to get started in research as an undergraduate but don't know where to start? Join us for an engaging panel discussion in collaboration with the Stony Brook iGEM Team featuring fellow undergraduates and recent grads who are actively involved in clinical/translational, molecular biology, chemistry, bioinformatics, psychology, and biomedical engineering research. Our panelists will share their personal experiences regarding how they found their respective opportunities, what their day-to-day looks like, the challenges they’ve faced, and the skills they’ve gained along the way. Whether you’re just starting out or looking to explore new opportunities, this is your chance to learn and connect! There will also be FREE pizza and snacks

events.name: SBU Annual ICPC Selection Contest 2025
events.descriptionPlain: SELECTION CONTEST ON OCT 10, 2025, with food and prizes! See the attached flyer. [ Please bring your own laptop, if possible ] The Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club (CompProg) is organizing a local ICPC-style Coding Contest on Friday, October 10, 2025 Room 2120 (with additional rooms as necessary) of Old Computer Science Building Contest hosted on HackerRank 5:00 - 9:00 PM Each participant must compete alone, and will get 7-8 algorithmic programming problems to solve in 3 hours. The goal is to identify the top candidates for our International Collegiate Programming Contest(ICPC) teams, which will participate in the Greater New York Regional Contest in the second week of November for a slot in the ICPC World Finals of 2026. Anyone with basic programming experience can take part and volunteers will help in explaining the format of the contest if you are not familiar with it. It is open to all SBU students. We will try to provide computers to students without their own laptop but it is not guaranteed. REGISTRATION: Registration for the contest is open now. Please register at: https://forms.gle/ d3xMzK9RaL2h8Jep9 Since the contest will be hosted on HackerRank, you must register a free account at www.hackerrank.com (which takes about a minute) if you have not already done so. Note that for your HackerRank account you must use exactly the same email address and first/last names that you will provide in the contest registration form above. SPONSOR: Stony Brook Computer Science Department PRIZES: There will be prizes for the top 3 finishers in the selection contest for each category (9 prizes in total) (1) Juniors (for first- and second-year undergraduates), (2) Seniors (for third- and fourth-year undergraduates), (3) Grads (for graduate students) OTHER INFO: For more information about ICPC, please check out http://www.acmgnyr.org . Problem sets used in our old selection contests can be found here: https://www3.cs. stonybrook.edu/~compprog/ . We also have a Google group for competitive programming: https://groups. google.com/a/cs.stonybrook. edu/d/forum/compprog Please join the group if you are interested in receiving future announcements. You can join our discord too. We look forward to seeing you at the contest! -- Saajid Chowdhury President SBU CompProg Website: http://cs.stonybrook. edu/~compprog/ To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an email to compprog+unsubscribe@cs. stonybrook.edu .
events.descriptionHtml:
SELECTION CONTEST ON OCT 10, 2025, with food and prizes!   See the attached flyer.

[ Please bring your own laptop, if possible ]

The Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club (CompProg) is organizing a local ICPC-style Coding Contest on

        Friday, October 10, 2025
        Room 2120 (with additional rooms as necessary) of Old Computer Science Building
        Contest hosted on HackerRank
        5:00 - 9:00 PM

Each participant must compete alone, and will get 7-8 algorithmic programming problems to solve in 3 hours.

The goal is to identify the top candidates for our International Collegiate Programming Contest(ICPC) teams, which will participate in the Greater New York Regional Contest in the second week of November for a slot in the ICPC World Finals of 2026.

Anyone with basic programming experience can take part and volunteers will help in explaining the format of the contest if you are not familiar with it. It is open to all SBU students.
 
We will try to provide computers to students without their own laptop but it is not guaranteed.

REGISTRATION:

Registration for the contest is open now. Please register at:

https://forms.gle/d3xMzK9RaL2h8Jep9
 
Since the contest will be hosted on HackerRank, you must register a free account at www.hackerrank.com (which takes about a minute) if you have not already done so. Note that for your HackerRank account you must use exactly the same email address and first/last names that you will provide in the contest registration form above.

SPONSOR:

Stony Brook Computer Science Department


PRIZES:

There will be prizes for the top 3 finishers in the selection contest for each category (9 prizes in total)

(1) Juniors (for first- and second-year undergraduates),
(2) Seniors (for third- and fourth-year undergraduates),
(3) Grads (for graduate students)


OTHER INFO:

For more information about ICPC, please check out http://www.acmgnyr.org.

Problem sets used in our old selection contests can be found here: https://www3.cs.stonybrook.edu/~compprog/.

We also have a Google group for competitive programming: https://groups.google.com/a/cs.stonybrook.edu/d/forum/compprog

Please join the group if you are interested in receiving future announcements. You can join our discord too. 

We look forward to seeing you at the contest!

--
Saajid Chowdhury
President
SBU CompProg
Website: http://cs.stonybrook.edu/~compprog/

 

To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an email to compprog+unsubscribe@cs.stonybrook.edu.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.descriptionPlain: Think you’d be a good world leader⁉️ Then join The Political Science Society’s Create Your Own Country event! In this GBM, you and your team will have the chance to craft a unique country of your choosing. Navigate various scenarios and challenges that will decide your country’s fate. May the best country win! 📥 For any questions, feel free to email us at thepoliticalsciencesociety@stonybrook.edu 📸 Follow our Instagram @sbupss Hope to see you all there!

events.name: Community @UNITI
events.descriptionPlain: Join USG and DICE in the UNITI Cultural Center for the 4th annual Community @UNITI 🎤☕️ Come through for a cozy night of open mic performances and karaoke, where everyone’s welcome to take the mic or just enjoy the vibes! We’ll have coffee, hot cocoa, donuts, and munchkins waiting for you 🍩☕️ Plus, don’t miss our raffle giveaway featuring fun prizes like a sun lamp, mini speaker, and the fan-favorite octopus plushie 🌞🔊🐙 Whether you’re performing, singing along, or just hanging out, Community @UNITI is all about creativity, laughter, and connection.✨ It’s always a campus favorite, hosted by VP of University Affairs Ece Naz Durali! See you Wednesday night at UNITI! 💫

events.name: Clubs & Orgs Leadership Council
events.descriptionPlain: Student Clubs & Organizations are invited to send one executive board representative from their club to participate in a monthly leadership council! This month, we will be giving club leaders the opportunity to mingle and discuss club business, while also participating in an activity with an opportunity to have a funded campus lifetime program! Student leadership representatives will be able to speak on their club experience and provide feedback directly to Student Engagement & Activities. Together with our department, we hope to build and improve our processes & policies, as well as provide the most up-to-date information to all club leaders.

events.name: NRHH GBM #4: Cocoa for Courage
events.descriptionPlain: Join NRHH for our biweekly GBM as we write letters for the Fire and Emergency Services team. We’ll also give out hot chocolate as we write letters. A warm drink to compliment a heartwarming letter for people that keep campus from being ablaze! There'll also be committee introductions by our E-Board members for our general body members to pick from and be a part of to make NRHH stronger than ever this school year! Meeting starts at 7PM on October 16th in the Student Activities Center Room 304! See y'all there :)

events.name: Sips & Sinners
events.descriptionPlain: When whispers in the night mix with the rhythm of the blues, some evils won’t stay buried... Join RA Rubbab for Sips & Sinners , an evening of cinematic chills & spiritual intrigue. Watch Sinners , a Southern Gothic horror-musical, then explore the mystique of Hoodoo with our self‐guided ‘Hoodoo Bar’ — herbs, roots, folklore, and mock-elixirs inspired by ritual that soothe the mind and spirit. What to expect: • Screening of Sinners (2025). • Hoodoo Bar — mix calming herbal tonics, learn about traditional rootwork & how certain rituals aim to center, protect, or heal. • Cozy lounge atmosphere — ambient lighting, gentle blues music, and space to reflect. When: Friday, October 16 @8PM Where: Lauterbur Classroom/Lounge Earn Wellness Credit for 2YLC Residents. Please Contact rubbab.syedjaffary@stonybrook.edu for any accessibility accommodations.

When whispers in the night mix with the rhythm of the blues, some evils won’t stay buried...

What to expect:
• Screening of Sinners (2025).

events.name: Mooncakes & Marigolds
events.descriptionPlain: Join the Community Action Network for a cross-cultural holiday celebration! We will have mooncakes and paper lantern decorating for Mid-Autumn Festival, rangoli and diya painting for Diwali, marigold garland making and sugar skull painting for Day of the Dead, and "apple-bobbing", pumpkin pie, and candy for Halloween. There will also be many fun raffles and prizes to win throughout the night. Bring a friend, or make some new ones as you drink apple cider and make fun crafts, while all raising money for charity! You won't want to miss it!

events.name: A Selenabration of Hispanic Heritage Month! Selena Movie & Craft Night
events.descriptionPlain: A Selenabration of Hispanic Heritage Month Saturday, October 18, 2025 | 7:00 PM – 10:00 PM Student Activities Center, Ballroom B Join us for a vibrant evening of music, nostalgia, and creativity as we honor Hispanic Heritage Month with A Selenabration dedicated to the Queen of Tejano, Selena Quintanilla-Pérez. Inspired by the beloved Selena movie, the night will feature themed crafts, throwback vibes, and an atmosphere filled with cultura and community. Come dressed in your best Selena-inspired look, enjoy make & take crafting activities, and sing along to the classics that continue to inspire generations. Whether you’re reliving the magic of the movie or discovering it for the first time, this night promises joy, connection, and celebration. This event is free and open to all Stony Brook students, faculty, and staff.
events.descriptionHtml:
A Selenabration of Hispanic Heritage Month
Saturday, October 18, 2025 | 7:00 PM – 10:00 PM
Student Activities Center, Ballroom B

Join us for a vibrant evening of music, nostalgia, and creativity as we honor Hispanic Heritage Month with A Selenabration dedicated to the Queen of Tejano, Selena Quintanilla-Pérez. Inspired by the beloved Selena movie, the night will feature themed crafts, throwback vibes, and an atmosphere filled with cultura and community.

Come dressed in your best Selena-inspired look, enjoy make & take crafting activities, and sing along to the classics that continue to inspire generations. Whether you’re reliving the magic of the movie or discovering it for the first time, this night promises joy, connection, and celebration.

This event is free and open to all Stony Brook students, faculty, and staff.

events.name: Carnaval del Barrio
events.descriptionPlain: Hispanic Heritage Month is celebrated each year to honor the histories, cultures, and contributions of Americans whose ancestors came from Spain, Mexico, the Caribbean, and Central and South America. This month-long celebration recognizes the rich traditions, diverse backgrounds, and vibrant cultures that have shaped and continue to enrich the United States Come and celebrate Hispanic Heritage Month with an event filled with music, dancing, live performances, games and activities, giveaways and food. Honoring Hispanic Heritage month through engaging in the culture, history, food and community.
events.descriptionHtml:
Hispanic Heritage Month is celebrated each year to honor the histories, cultures, and contributions of Americans whose ancestors came from Spain, Mexico, the Caribbean, and Central and South America.

This month-long celebration recognizes the rich traditions, diverse backgrounds, and vibrant cultures that have shaped and continue to enrich the United States

Come and celebrate Hispanic Heritage Month with an event filled with music, dancing, live performances, games and activities, giveaways and food. Honoring Hispanic Heritage month through engaging in the culture, history, food and community.

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: Kevin Yager Guest Speech by Brookhaven National Laboratory
events.descriptionPlain: Brookhaven National Laboratory: The Future of AI-Empowered Science 📆 Date: Monday, October 20 🕓 Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM 📍 Location: SAC 302 🗣 Speaker: Kevin Yager, CFN Interim Director at BNL Students will have the opportunity to hear from Dr. Kevin Yager, Director of the Center for Functional Nanomaterials (CFN) at Brookhaven National Laboratory and a leading voice in AI-driven scientific research. Dr. Yager will discuss how cutting-edge artificial intelligence is transforming scientific discovery—from autonomous experiments to accelerated materials design—and what this means for the future of research and innovation. The event will feature: • 🎤 Talk: A presentation by Dr. Yager on the future of AI-empowered science • ❓ Q&A: An open forum for student questions • 🤝 Networking: A chance to personally speak with Dr. Yager after the session • 🍕 Food: Pizza and soda will be served! All students are welcome—no prior AI experience required.
events.descriptionHtml:
Brookhaven National Laboratory: The Future of AI-Empowered Science

📆 Date: Monday, October 20

🕓 Time: 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM

📍 Location: SAC 302

🗣 Speaker: Kevin Yager, CFN Interim Director at BNL

Students will have the opportunity to hear from Dr. Kevin Yager, Director of the Center for Functional Nanomaterials (CFN) at Brookhaven National Laboratory and a leading voice in AI-driven scientific research.

Dr. Yager will discuss how cutting-edge artificial intelligence is transforming scientific discovery—from autonomous experiments to accelerated materials design—and what this means for the future of research and innovation.

The event will feature:

• 🎤 Talk: A presentation by Dr. Yager on the future of AI-empowered science

• ❓ Q&A: An open forum for student questions

• 🤝 Networking: A chance to personally speak with Dr. Yager after the session

• 🍕 Food: Pizza and soda will be served!

All students are welcome—no prior AI experience required.

events.name: Naga-Night: Fireball Festival
events.descriptionPlain: The Naga Fireball Festival, or Bang Fai Phaya Nak, is an annual Thai event typically held in northeastern Thailand, where glowing red orbs rise from the Mekong River. Locals believe the fireballs are exhaled by the Naga, a mythical river serpent. In celebration of the Naga Fireball Festival, Baan Thai Club is hosting its own "fireball" festival. This event will have performances featuring other student organizations while our general body can participate in activities and fun games hosted by BTC. Food will also be catered from Raan Thai.
