events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: Red Watch Band Training *Homecoming Special with F&S Life*
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for this Homecoming Special Red Watch Band co hosted by Fraternity and Sorority Life to learn how to identify an overdose and how to call for help to save a life! You must register for this training at tinyurl.com/cpobystander There will be free pizza for all attendees right after the training! To earn the Upstander Award (includes a certificate and graduation cord), you will need to complete the following: May 2026 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR and Green Dot by January 1, 2026 May 2027 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR and Green Dot by January 1, 2027 May 2028 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR, Green Dot AND Hands-Only CPR by January 1, 2028 May 2029 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR, Green Dot AND Hands-Only CPR by January 1, 2029 For accessibility needs please contact prevention_outreach@stonybrook.edu
events.descriptionHtml:
Join us for this Homecoming Special Red Watch Band co hosted by Fraternity and Sorority Life to learn how to identify an overdose and how to call for help to save a life!

 

You must register for this training at tinyurl.com/cpobystander

 

There will be free pizza for all attendees right after the training! 

 

 

To earn the Upstander Award (includes a certificate and graduation cord), you will need to complete the following: 

May 2026 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR and Green Dot by January 1, 2026

May 2027 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR and Green Dot by January 1, 2027

May 2028 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR, Green Dot AND Hands-Only CPR by January 1, 2028

May 2029 graduates: complete Red Watch Band, QPR, Green Dot AND Hands-Only CPR by January 1, 2029

 

For accessibility needs please contact prevention_outreach@stonybrook.edu

events.name: Level Up Your Cyber Game
events.descriptionPlain: Hello Seawolves, October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, and you're invited to a fun, interactive event designed to boost your cybersecurity knowledge — and give you a chance to win some awesome prizes! Put your cyber smarts to the test in a fast-paced, game-style competition. We'll have free pizza, snacks, and swag giveaways for the winners and participants. Whether you're a cybersecurity expert or just starting to learn, this is a great opportunity to have fun while learning how to stay safe online. Come with friends or come solo — just don’t miss out! All students are welcome, and no prior experience is needed. We look forward to seeing you there!
events.descriptionHtml:
Hello Seawolves,

 

October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, and you're invited to a fun, interactive event designed to boost your cybersecurity knowledge — and give you a chance to win some awesome prizes!

Put your cyber smarts to the test in a fast-paced, game-style competition. We'll have free pizza, snacks, and swag giveaways for the winners and participants. Whether you're a cybersecurity expert or just starting to learn, this is a great opportunity to have fun while learning how to stay safe online.

Come with friends or come solo — just don’t miss out! All students are welcome, and no prior experience is needed.

We look forward to seeing you there!

events.descriptionPlain: Practice self care and make some flu season kits to prepare yourself for the start of this cold season! If you think you can tough out this trying time...good luck to you. For the rest of us, there will be many teas, vitamin packets, chapsticks, honey sticks, stickers, and other goodies to choose from that will aid in staying warm and protected. Keep in mind that supplies are limited and will be available on a first come first served basis, so get here early or prepare to envy everyone else who can still breathe through their nose🤧

events.name: PHSRG Monthly Member Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Upcoming Meeting: we have scheduled our second monthly meeting for Wednesday, October 22nd at 4:30 pm in HSC 2-272AB and a virtual option is also included. The details have been posted under the "Monthly Meetings" module. We will have a panel of current/former PhD students and graduate students from the Program in Public Health to share their research journeys. This will be followed by a quick brainstorming session in small groups to identify research interests and identify sub-committees for specific topics. Lastly, we will review once more the executive board positions available that need to be fulfilled and review the agenda for our next meeting in early November.

events.name: Emerging Talent Networking Dinner
events.descriptionPlain: Register on Handshake ! Thursday, October 23, 2025 6pm - 8pm SAC Ballroom A Looking to build connections with top employers and explore exciting career opportunities? Join us for the Emerging Talent Networking Dinner , a signature in-person event designed to help you engage directly with professionals from a range of industries who are eager to connect with Stony Brook’s emerging talent. This unique roundtable dinner format allows you to rotate among employer tables throughout the evening—giving you the chance to introduce yourself, ask questions, and gain insights into a variety of companies, roles, and career paths. Whether you're exploring internships, part-time roles, or full-time positions, this is a great opportunity to expand your network and make a memorable impression. Event Highlights: Enjoy dinner while participating in small-group conversations with professionals Rotate tables to maximize your exposure to different employers Hear from a keynote speaker who will share career inspiration and insight Learn about current openings and career pathways directly from company reps Engage with campus student organizations co-hosting the event Wrap up with open networking alongside students, alumni, and employers Come prepared with questions, your resume, and your curiosity—this is your chance to shine in a professional yet approachable setting.
events.descriptionHtml:
Register on Handshake!

Thursday, October 23, 2025
6pm - 8pm
SAC Ballroom A

Looking to build connections with top employers and explore exciting career opportunities? Join us for the Emerging Talent Networking Dinner, a signature in-person event designed to help you engage directly with professionals from a range of industries who are eager to connect with Stony Brook’s emerging talent.

This unique roundtable dinner format allows you to rotate among employer tables throughout the evening—giving you the chance to introduce yourself, ask questions, and gain insights into a variety of companies, roles, and career paths. Whether you're exploring internships, part-time roles, or full-time positions, this is a great opportunity to expand your network and make a memorable impression.

Event Highlights:

  • Enjoy dinner while participating in small-group conversations with professionals

  • Rotate tables to maximize your exposure to different employers

  • Hear from a keynote speaker who will share career inspiration and insight

  • Learn about current openings and career pathways directly from company reps

  • Engage with campus student organizations co-hosting the event

  • Wrap up with open networking alongside students, alumni, and employers

 

Come prepared with questions, your resume, and your curiosity—this is your chance to shine in a professional yet approachable setting.

events.name: Toy Adaptation
events.descriptionPlain: Toy Adaptation Event is a hands-on workshop where students will team up with industry professionals to modify commercially available toys to make them accessible for children with physical and cognitive disabilities. Participants will learn the basics of circuit wiring, soldering, and toy mechanics in a collaborative and educational setting. Adapted toys will be donated to families and local therapy centers/schools. This event is aimed at raising awareness around assistive technology and inclusive design. No prior experience is required — we will provide all the materials, tools, and guidance needed.

events.name: Yo Soy un Seawolf Homecoming Social
events.descriptionPlain: YO SOY UN SEAWOLF SOCIAL Cultura, Familia, y Comunidad Saturday, October 25th, 2025 | 5:30 PM –7:30 PM ET | UNITI Cultural Center - SB Union 113 This year, Stony Brook University proudly celebrates 36 years of Hispanic Heritage Month (HHM)—the first and longest-running heritage month at SBU, which has paved the way for more affinity groups to be part of the University’s rich cultural legacy. Join the Latin American Seawolves community for refreshments and meaningful opportunities to connect with alumni, faculty, staff, and current students. Open to all! Family and friends, bienvenidos! This event is free and open to all. Register Here Co-Sponsored by: Hispanic Heritage Month Committee, Universitarios Unidos Latinoamericano (Latin American Faculty & Staff Resource Group), Stony Brook University Alumni Association, Office of Diversity, Inclusion, Intercultural Initiatives (DI3), and Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE)

events.descriptionPlain: 🎮 🎃 WELCOME TO YOUR NIGHT SHIFT AT SPOOKY BROOK 🎃 The cameras flicker. The power’s low. events.descriptionPlain: Ditch the enrollment stress and prepare for your best semester yet! We'll demystify everything from navigating Add/Drop/Swap and understanding those pesky Holds to becoming a pro with the Schedule Builder . events.name: Advanced Python - Internal Competition Round 2
events.descriptionPlain: Put your skills to the test in a fun, hands-on Reinforcement Learning challenge. Design and train AI agents to play Snake or solve a Rubik’s Cube—learning through trial and error, just like a human would. Compete with others to see whose model performs best, with $200 worth of prizes for the top performers. Whether you’re aiming to win or just want to experiment with RL, this is the perfect playground. Round Details: Round 1 - Intro to RL: It's a Me, RL! | October 15th, Frey 205 Round 2 - Advanced RL: Hungry Python | October 27th, SAC 302 Round 3 - Advanced RL: Car Racing RL Challenge | November 5th, Frey 205 *All rounds are from 12:30PM-1:50PM

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: GradConnect Series
events.descriptionPlain: We are excited to invite graduate students to the October GradConnect Series , organized by the Graduate School. Th is new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community. For the October GradConnect Series event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students. Recreation and Wellness Student Accessibility and Support Center (SASC) Student Health Services (SHS) Light refreshments will be provided. Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December! Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event .
events.descriptionHtml:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the October GradConnect Series, organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the October GradConnect Series event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students.

 

  • Recreation and Wellness 
  • Student Accessibility and Support Center (SASC) 
  • Student Health Services (SHS)

 

 

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  

 

Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event.
events.reservations: Optional RSVP on Campus Engage · 3 students have RSVP’d.

events.name: iGrad: Intercultural Communication and Professional Etiquette
events.descriptionPlain: This session is designed to equip international graduate students with the essential interpersonal and professional skills necessary for success in a globalized workplace. The workshop will cover key aspects of professional etiquette in a Western context, including proper handshaking, exchanging business cards, and the art of networking. Participants will learn how to craft an effective elevator pitch, communicate their professional goals clearly and concisely, and build confidence in professional settings. The goal is to bridge cultural gaps and provide students with the tools to navigate professional environments with poise and competence. Presented by Jessica Feliz, Director of International Graduate Programs for the College of Business.

events.name: Point72/Cubist Systematic Strategies Information Panel
events.descriptionPlain: Register on Handshake ! Point72/ Cubist Systematic Strategies will be hosting a Lunch and Learn on Wednesday, October 29h at (SAC 302). Sign up to meet with researchers and recruiters, learn more about internship and full-time opportunities at Cubist, pick up the latest swag, and of course enjoy lunch! Cubist designs and implements systematic, computer-driven trading strategies based on rigorous research to invest across multiple liquid asset classes. Our teams around the world develop their investment theses from our extensive access to a wide range of publicly available data. Our colleagues come from other firms, related fields, and directly from academia. Whatever your background, here we all share a passion for problem solving, strong technical skills, and a commitment to the highest professional and ethical standards.

events.name: Tabler's Terror Fest 👻
events.descriptionPlain: Tabler's Terror Fest is an annual Halloween-themed event hosted by the Tabler's Quad Connex at Stony Brook University. This event is held in the Tabler Black Box Theater featuring a Haunted Walk where students can navigate through different rooms filled with monsters attempting to scare you. Attendees can also enjoy a costume contest, win prizes through Bingo, and indulge in candy throughout the night. The event offers a festive atmosphere with karaoke, popcorn, photobooths, and other mini games to weed out your boredom. Come celebrate our popular gathering that kicks off the Halloweekend celebrations on campus.

events.name: Haunted Halloween Health Fair
events.descriptionPlain: Join the Cardiology Student Association (CSA) and Stony Brook’s pre-health community for a thrilling night of fun, fright, and fundraising! The Haunted Health Fair combines Halloween festivities with interactive, health-themed games and activities hosted by various pre-health organizations across campus. From dissecting “pumpkin hearts” and diagnosing “haunted patients” to crafting bubbling health potions and learning zombie first aid, this event puts a spooky spin on science and medicine! Attendees can enjoy festive music, candy, and photo ops, while competing in our costume contest and raffle giveaways for a chance to win exciting prizes. All proceeds will support CSA’s heart-health awareness initiatives and collaborative community outreach efforts with other pre-health clubs. Don’t miss out on this one-of-a-kind experience that brings together students passionate about healthcare, education, and giving back, with a haunting twist!

From dissecting “pumpkin hearts” and diagnosing “haunted patients” to crafting bubbling health potions and learning zombie first aid, this event puts a spooky spin on science and medicine! Attendees can enjoy festive music, candy, and photo ops, while competing in our costume contest and raffle giveaways for a chance to win exciting prizes.

All proceeds will support CSA’s heart-health awareness initiatives and collaborative community outreach efforts with other pre-health clubs. Don’t miss out on this one-of-a-kind experience that brings together students passionate about healthcare, education, and giving back, with a haunting twist!

events.name: Hall-O-Jinn
events.descriptionPlain: Pakistani Student Association is hosting Hallo-O-Jinn, a Halloween event that also highlights Pakistani culture. The goal is to create a fun and engaging space where students can share the supernatural creature stories and folktales many of us grew up hearing, along with personal experiences tied to them. It will be both entertaining and cultural, giving students the chance to connect, learn from each other, and build community in a relaxed setting. This event will encourage interaction, allow students to celebrate Halloween with a cultural twist, and provide a unique way for people to meet and engage with each other.

events.name: Halloween Discoteka
events.descriptionPlain: Get ready to turn up at Discoteka — the ultimate Halloween dance party on campus! 💃🕺 Step into the Shore Club for a night packed with music, energy, and nonstop vibes that’ll keep you moving all night long. ⚡ 🔥 DJ Nic , one of Stony Brook’s top campus DJs, will be throwing down the beats — we’re bringing that Brighton Beach-style nightlife right to campus: flashy, fun, and just the right amount of chaos. 😎 Sip on spooky mocktails 🍹, grab some killer food 🍴, and load up on candy 🍬 to fuel your night. Don’t forget your costume — the most creative and spook-tacular fits will take home some epic prizes! 🏆👻 🎃 RSVP is required to get priority for food and treats — spots fill up fast, so don’t sleep on it! Bring your crew, dress to impress, and get ready for a night full of lights, beats, and good vibes . 💥 This isn’t your average Halloween party — it’s Discoteka , and it’s gonna be legendary . 🚨

events.name: PING PONG TOURNAMENT
events.descriptionPlain: Resident Ping Pong Tournament ! This event is designed for residents of all skill levels, whether you’re a seasoned player or trying ping pong for the first time. Come join us for an afternoon of friendly competition, community bonding, and lots of fun. The tournament will take place in Chapin MPR, with multiple tables set up to ensure everyone gets plenty of time to play. Matches will be fast-paced and exciting, and winners will advance through the brackets until we crown the ultimate ping pong champion. Don’t worry if you’re new to the game—we’ll have volunteers on hand to explain the rules and give tips to help you improve your serve and returns. Beyond the competition, this is a great opportunity to meet fellow residents, make new friends, and enjoy a lively, energetic atmosphere. Snacks and refreshments will be provided to keep your energy up while you battle it out for bragging rights and prizes.

events.name: 🎬🍿 Chills & Thrills Movie Night! 🎃
events.descriptionPlain: 🎬🍿 Chills & Thrills Movie Night! 🎃 Join us for a spooky-good movie night featuring jump scares, laughs, and cozy vibes. While you watch, grab free popcorn & drinks 🥤🍿 and pick up our bite-size info pamphlets that unpack the psychology of watching movies —from why we love fear to how sound and lighting mess with our brains 🧠✨. The handouts are tied to PSY 103: Introduction to Psychology (a common SBC course for first-years), so it’s fun and a little academic-friendly. 📚 📍 James Lounge 📅 Tuesday, Oct 28 @ 7 pm 👥 Open to everyone—bring friends, blankets, and your best spooky spirit! 🧟‍♀️🕯️ See you there… if you dare. 😈🎥

events.name: Be the Voice of Your Generation: A FirstGen Panel & Brunch
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for a delicious brunch and an inspiring conversation with FirstGen faculty, staff, administrators, and students, with real stories, honest advice, and practical tips for navigating college as a first-generation student. Whether you're seeking guidance or simply want to feel part of a community that understands, this event is for you. We are excited to have our panelist share their journey and wisdom. Dr. Celia Marshik | Dean of the Graduate School and Vice Provost for Graduate and Professional Education Dr. Ryan Braun | Assistant Provost for Academic Advising, Division of Undergraduate Education Giselle Castro | Student, School of Social Welfare Isabelle Irizarry-McAdam | Student, Biochemistry major and Theatre Arts minor Cenia Larios | Student, School of Social Welfare Saniya Lopez | Student, Business Management and Pre-Law, Resident Assistant and Peer Career Coach Jose Romero-Diaz | Student, School of Social Welfare Also, stop by the various resource tables to connect with SB offices and learn the ways you can find support as a First-Generation College Student. For more information and to see more events, visit stonybrook.edu/firstgen
events.descriptionHtml:

events.name: Dia de los Muertos | Day of the Dead Celebration
events.descriptionPlain: Dia de Los Muertos | Day of the Dead Celebration Monday, November 3rd, 2025 7:00-9:00 PM | Student Activities Center, Ballroom A Join us as we honor and celebrate Día de Los Muertos (Day of the Dead) — a vibrant cultural tradition that pays tribute to the lives and memories of our loved ones who have passed. Although Dia de los Muertos is celebrated throughout Latin America, it has deep pre-colonial indigenous roots. Guests can take part in traditional crafts, including papel picado, mask painting, and tissue paper flowers, and are invited to contribute photos or small offerings to a community ofrenda (altar) in remembrance of family, friends, or cultural icons who have inspired them. This celebration will feature a special workshop led by the Calpulli Mexican Dance Company . Come early to sign up for the limited workshop spots . Enjoy Latin American refreshments as we come together to celebrate life, legacy, and community through art, dance, and shared stories. This event is free and open to all. Sponsored by: Hispanic Heritage Month Committee (HHM) Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE) Latin American and Caribbean Studies Center (LACS) Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature (HLL) Mexican Student Organization (MEXSO) Latin American Student Organization (LASO) International Student Organization (ISO)
events.descriptionHtml:
Dia de Los Muertos | Day of the Dead Celebration
Monday, November 3rd, 2025
7:00-9:00 PM | Student Activities Center, Ballroom A

Join us as we honor and celebrate Día de Los Muertos (Day of the Dead) — a vibrant cultural tradition that pays tribute to the lives and memories of our loved ones who have passed. Although Dia de los Muertos is celebrated throughout Latin America, it has deep pre-colonial indigenous roots.

Guests can take part in traditional crafts, including papel picado, mask painting, and tissue paper flowers, and are invited to contribute photos or small offerings to a community ofrenda (altar) in remembrance of family, friends, or cultural icons who have inspired them. 

This celebration will feature a special workshop led by the Calpulli Mexican Dance Company. Come early to sign up for the limited workshop spots.

Enjoy Latin American refreshments as we come together to celebrate life, legacy, and community through art, dance, and shared stories.  
This event is free and open to all.

Sponsored by:

  • Hispanic Heritage Month Committee (HHM)
  • Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement (DICE)
  • Latin American and Caribbean Studies Center (LACS)
  • Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature (HLL)
  • Mexican Student Organization (MEXSO)
  • Latin American Student Organization (LASO)
  • International Student Organization (ISO)

events.name: Election Day Celebration
events.descriptionPlain: Celebrate Election Day with the Center for Civic Justice, this November 4th! Join us on Election Day from 8AM to 8PM at the Union Patio for a full day of Election Day programming and information. Representatives from the Center for Civic Justice will be available to provide last-minute assistance to help campus community members successfully cast their ballot. Additionally, each hour will feature a takeover hosted in collaboration with numerous community partners. Themes range from crafting to stress relief, to food and fun. These events will offer a chance to connect and celebrate the democratic process, and appreciate our ability to be a part of it! The schedule for Election Day is: Bagels and Ballots | 8AM - 10AM Seawolves Pantry Information Station | 10AM-11AM Pizza at the Polls | 11AM - 1PM Fraternity and Sorority Life: "From Greek Row to Capital Hill" | 1PM-2PM Donuts for Democracy | 2PM-4PM Destress with the Student Support Team and CPO | 4PM-5PM Sip and Submit your Ballot with Hot Chocolate | 5PM - 8PM Students who live off-campus can still join us from 8:00AM to 8:00PM for a day full of activities including giveaways, crafts, Uncle Sam, photo ops, "I Voted" stickers and more!
events.descriptionHtml:
Celebrate Election Day with the Center for Civic Justice, this November 4th!

Join us on Election Day from 8AM to 8PM at the Union Patio for a full day of Election Day programming and information. Representatives from the Center for Civic Justice will be available to provide last-minute assistance to help campus community members successfully cast their ballot.

Additionally, each hour will feature a takeover hosted in collaboration with numerous community partners. Themes range from crafting to stress relief, to food and fun. These events will offer a chance to connect and celebrate the democratic process, and appreciate our ability to be a part of it! Seawolves Pantry Information Station | 10AM-11AM

Fraternity and Sorority Life: "From Greek Row to Capital Hill" | 1PM-2PM

Destress with the Student Support Team and CPO | 4PM-5PM

Students who live off-campus can still join us from 8:00AM to 8:00PM for a day full of activities including giveaways, crafts, Uncle Sam, photo ops, "I Voted" stickers and more!

events.name: Ecology and Evolutions Society Club: From Mouths to Mangroves (Microbial Ecology)
events.descriptionPlain: Ever wonder how tiny microbes shape the world around us? Come to our event, "Microbial Ecology: From Mouths to Mangroves" to explore how microorganisms live everywhere—from our own mouths to muddy mangrove forests! Our speaker will share cool stories from their research on the human oral microbiome and a microbe that helps break down detritus in mangrove soil. They’ll also talk a bit about their path in science and taking time between undergrad and grad school. Come learn, ask questions, and see why microbial ecology is way more exciting than you might think!

events.name: OpenAI x WebDev: Build an AI Interviewer
events.descriptionPlain: Build an AI-Powered Technical Interviewer Join us for a hands-on workshop where you’ll create a real-time, AI-driven interviewer that listens, analyzes, and responds — just like a real technical recruiter! 🧠 What You’ll Learn 🎙️ Stream microphone input directly to GPT ⚡ Process live responses for real-time interview simulation 💻 Integrate with a coding environment for mock technical challenges 🧰 Tech Stack Backend: FastAPI • OpenAI SDK • WebSockets Frontend: Next.js (React) • TypeScript • Tailwind CSS Dev Tools: Docker • Poetry • GitHub Codespaces (no local setup needed!) 🧩 Why You Should Join Instantly spin up a browser-based development environment Build a full-stack AI project from scratch Learn modern tools for real-time GPT integration 🍪 Snacks will be provided! Whether you’re into AI , real-time systems , or modern full-stack dev , this workshop is for you. 👉 Come code with us and bring your interviewer to life!
events.descriptionHtml:
Build an AI-Powered Technical Interviewer

Join us for a hands-on workshop where you’ll create a real-time, AI-driven interviewer that listens, analyzes, and responds — just like a real technical recruiter!

🧠 What You’ll Learn

  • 🎙️ Stream microphone input directly to GPT

  • ⚡ Process live responses for real-time interview simulation

  • 💻 Integrate with a coding environment for mock technical challenges

🧰 Tech Stack

Backend: FastAPI • OpenAI SDK • WebSockets
Frontend: Next.js (React) • TypeScript • Tailwind CSS
Dev Tools: Docker • Poetry • GitHub Codespaces (no local setup needed!)

🧩 Why You Should Join

  • Instantly spin up a browser-based development environment

  • Build a full-stack AI project from scratch

  • Learn modern tools for real-time GPT integration

  • 🍪 Snacks will be provided!

Whether you’re into AI, real-time systems, or modern full-stack dev, this workshop is for you.

👉 Come code with us and bring your interviewer to life!

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: E&E Colloquium: "The Building Blocks of Vertebrate Diversity" by Dr. Laura Alencar
events.descriptionPlain: Vertebrates range from ray-finned fish to salamanders to humans—so what drives this incredible diversity? Dr. Laura Alencar seeks to answer this very question in her talk, "The Building Blocks of Vertebrate Diversity." She will explore the key innovations that make this species so diverse and how these factors accelerates or constrain evolution. If you are interested in learning more about vertebrates then you won't want to miss this. Date: Wednesday, November 5, 2025 | 2:15 PM Location: Life Sciences Building, Room 038 Don’t miss it if you're curious about what makes vertebrates so unique!

This movie night is more than entertainment, it’s an exploration of civic courage , representation , and the ongoing pursuit of equity . Email oyshe.khan@stonybrook.edu for any questions, concerns, or accessibility needs.

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.
events.descriptionHtml:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: PPGA's Movie Night: Heightened Scrutiny
events.descriptionPlain: Join us to watch the film Heightened Scrutiny! "Fearless civil rights lawyer Chase Strangio battles at the Supreme Court for transgender adolescents’ access to gender-affirming healthcare, confronting not only the legal system but also a media landscape that distorts public perception and threatens the struggle for trans rights. With the dangerous SCOTUS decision upholding the ban on life-saving healthcare, HEIGHTENED SCRUTINY is an urgent call to action against bigotry and injustice." -https://www.heightenedscrutinydoc.com/

events.name: Tea Across the Globe!!
events.descriptionPlain: Celebrate Culture. Share Stories. Sip Together. Take a trip around the world—one cup of tea at a time! 🌎✨ Join us for Tea Around the World , a cozy cultural experience where we’ll sample teas from different countries and explore the stories, traditions, and people behind them. From Japanese matcha and Moroccan mint to Indian chai and British breakfast blends, each sip will transport you to a different part of the globe. Residents and international students will share fun cultural facts, personal experiences, and even a few musical moments from their home countries. This event is all about connection — discovering the beauty of diversity, learning something new, and appreciating how one simple drink can bring people together across borders. Enjoy light snacks, music from around the world, and great company in a warm, welcoming atmosphere. Whether you’re a tea lover or just looking for meaningful conversations, you won’t want to miss this evening of flavor, friendship, and cultural appreciation.

events.name: 🧀 Queso & Questions❓
events.descriptionPlain: Get ready for a burst of flavor and fun at Queso & Questions, the ultimate trivia balloon pop challenge! Here's how it works: - 🎯Throw a Dart — Score enough points for the designated Balloon - 🎈 Pop a Balloon — Each balloon hides a mystery question inside. - 🧠 Answer It Right — Show off your smarts and tackle the trivia! - 🎉 Win a Prize — Flip the paper to reveal your reward: every correct answer earns you the prize printed on the back! From a nostalgic Tom & Jerry keychain set to some cool SBU Merch, every balloon holds a chance to win something exciting. Whether you're a queso connoisseur or a trivia master, this event is your chance to snack, laugh, and score big.

events.name: Living Textbook
events.descriptionPlain: Join the Skincare & Wellness Club and Seawolves Accessibility Network for the first annual Living Textbook, an interactive event connecting individuals living with chronic illnesses and disabilities with listeners in an open dialogue about lived experience, accessibility, and patient-centered care. Volunteers who share their story will be paired with a small group of listeners for 15-20 minute discussions. Attendees can expect to come away with a better understanding of the day-to-day experiences of individuals with chronic illness and disability that can't always be gathered from a textbook. Island Empanada catering will be provided. Interested in sharing your story? Register here . Alternate link: https://forms.gle/pkHEjJ4Sf8xLfrhD7 For questions or accessibility concerns please contact sbu.skincare@gmail.com

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: GBM #4: S&P Global - Machine Learning in Finance
events.descriptionPlain: 💡Curious how Artificial Intelligence is transforming the world of finance? Join us for lunch 🥪 and an engaging panel discussion with industry experts from S&P Global 📊, where we’ll dive deep into how AI and data-driven models are transforming everything from market analysis to risk management 💰🤖 🎙️Our panelists will share their insights on: What it’s really like to apply machine learning in finance, from predictive modeling to natural language processing 🖥️ The skills and mindsets that helped them succeed in this rapidly evolving field The real-world challenges of working with complex, ever-changing financial data 📋 How to build a career at the intersection of AI and finance, and what students can do right now to get started 💼 ✨ Whether you’re passionate about finance, data science, or AI, this event is your chance to gain first-hand knowledge and advice from leaders at the forefront of financial innovation 🌍📈
events.descriptionHtml:
💡Curious how Artificial Intelligence is transforming the world of finance? Join us for lunch 🥪 and an engaging panel discussion with industry experts from S&P Global 📊, where we’ll dive deep into how AI and data-driven models are transforming everything from market analysis to risk management 💰🤖

🎙️Our panelists will share their insights on:

  • What it’s really like to apply machine learning in finance, from predictive modeling to natural language processing 🖥️
  • The skills and mindsets that helped them succeed in this rapidly evolving field 
  • The real-world challenges of working with complex, ever-changing financial data 📋
  • How to build a career at the intersection of AI and finance, and what students can do right now to get started 💼

✨ Whether you’re passionate about finance, data science, or AI, this event is your chance to gain first-hand knowledge and advice from leaders at the forefront of financial innovation 🌍📈

events.name: The Voices of Humanity Showcase
events.descriptionPlain: Come join the Humanities Institute for a showcase to honor the winners and finalists of "The Voices of Humanity" competition and celebrate our shared humanity. There will be a gallery walk, and the winners and finalists will discuss their creative processes. A reception with free food and free Humanities Institute merchandise will be available! Attendees will be eligible for a piece of work (art, writing, music, audio, multimedia); the piece of work with the most votes from attendees will be considered for the people's choice award. events.name: Native American Heritage Month Celebration & Panel Discussion
events.descriptionPlain: 2025 Native American Heritage Month Celebration & PANEL DISCUSSION Wednesday, November 12, 2025 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM | Zuccaire Gallery, Staller Center Join us for Stony Brook University’s 2025 Native American Heritage Month Celebration! The event will feature a spoken word performance by Sumaq Kilari, a viewing of the Zuccaire Gallery exhibition Weaving Words, Weaving Worlds: The Power of Indigenous Language in Contemporary Art , and a Panel Discussion with faculty from Native American and Indigenous Studies (NAIS). Panel Discussion Moderator: Joseph Pierce, Director of NAIS and Associate Professor, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature Panelists: David Weiden, Department of English Valeria Meiller, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature Vick Quezada, Department of Art Darcey Evans, Department of Anthropology Reception with light refreshments to follow. THIS EVENT IS FREE AND OPEN TO ALL. Presented in collaboration with Native American and Indigenous Studies Initiative, the Office of Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement, and the Paul W. Zuccaire Gallery
events.descriptionHtml:
2025 Native American Heritage Month Celebration & PANEL DISCUSSION
Wednesday, November 12, 2025
4:00 PM - 6:00 PM | Zuccaire Gallery, Staller Center

Join us for Stony Brook University’s 2025 Native American Heritage Month Celebration!

The event will feature a spoken word performance by Sumaq Kilari, a viewing of the Zuccaire Gallery exhibition Weaving Words, Weaving Worlds: The Power of Indigenous Language in Contemporary Art, and a Panel Discussion with faculty from Native American and Indigenous Studies (NAIS). 

Panel Discussion Moderator: Joseph Pierce, Director of NAIS and Associate Professor, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature

Panelists:

  • David Weiden, Department of English
  • Valeria Meiller, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literature
  • Vick Quezada, Department of Art
  • Darcey Evans, Department of Anthropology

Reception with light refreshments to follow. THIS EVENT IS FREE AND OPEN TO ALL.

Presented in collaboration with Native American and Indigenous Studies Initiative, the Office of Diversity, Intercultural and Community Engagement, and the Paul W. Zuccaire Gallery

events.name: GSO Top Golf 2025
events.descriptionPlain: Ready to unwind, swing some clubs, and share a few laughs? Join us for an afternoon of fun at Top Golf on Sunday, November 16, 2025 , from 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM at 5231 Express Dr N, Holtsville, NY 11742 . No experience needed - just show up, take your shot, and enjoy the company of fellow graduate students. You’ll get free snacks and drinks , and plenty of chances to connect, relax, and maybe even discover your hidden golf skills. Please note: No Transportation will be provided , so plan your travel in advance and arrive by 11:45 AM to check in. Registration details: A registration form will be shared with all graduate students via email. Only 150 participants will be selected randomly , so make sure to register before the deadline for a chance to join!

events.name: Modern Art Competition
events.descriptionPlain: Have you ever wanted to make the silliest or most interesting art to blow people’s minds? If so, then come to RA Lennie and RA Edward’s Modern Art Contest on 11/16/2025 at 8:15PM @ West E Commons and create whatever your imagination thinks of. Feel free to bring whatever you need! Try your best to make your work stand out and tell its own unique story! We will be evaluating art based on a few categories: Most thought-provoking (make people wonder about your creation!) - How unique the art is (get creative and make your work stand out from the others!) - Story telling (a good story always gives a new interesting perspective. Make your art tell your own unique story!) There will be chips and drinks available if you stop by! Hope to see you there! Any questions? Send an email to: lennie.gleyzer@stonybrook.edu or edward.cibu@stonybrook.edu

events.name: E&E Seminar: Evolutionary Ecology of Malaria Parasites in a Changing World
events.descriptionPlain: Malaria has reshaped our world through its many victims, but how has it shaped its hosts? Harvard researcher and disease ecology expert Benjamin L. Rice will present a fascinating talk, “Evolutionary Ecology of Malaria Parasites in a Changing World.” Malaria—one of the most influential diseases of the modern era—has shaped not only the lives of its victims but also the evolutionary trajectories of its hosts. Dr. Rice will explore the core evolutionary dynamics of these parasites and what they mean for our world moving forward. If any of this interests you, you won’t want to miss his talk! ⏰ When: Nov. 17 | 12:30 PM – 1:45 PM 🗺 Where: Laufer Center
events.descriptionHtml:
Malaria has reshaped our world through its many victims, but how has it shaped its hosts?

Harvard researcher and disease ecology expert Benjamin L. Rice will present a fascinating talk, “Evolutionary Ecology of Malaria Parasites in a Changing World.” Malaria—one of the most influential diseases of the modern era—has shaped not only the lives of its victims but also the evolutionary trajectories of its hosts. Dr. Rice will explore the core evolutionary dynamics of these parasites and what they mean for our world moving forward. If any of this interests you, you won’t want to miss his talk!

⏰ When: Nov. 17 | 12:30 PM – 1:45 PM
🗺 Where: Laufer Center

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.
events.descriptionHtml:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: Living World Lecture Series: "The Afterlife of Roundup: Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health"
events.descriptionPlain: 🌍 Living World Presents: The Afterlife of Roundup — Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health 🌱 The world beneath our feet is alive — teeming with hidden communities and remarkable resilience. In our next Living World event, Dr. Sharon Pochron will reveal how even the soil itself tells a story of survival and adaptation. Join us as Dr. Pochron shares her research on the effects of Roundup and explores what happens beneath the surface in “ The Afterlife of Roundup: Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health .” Discover how soil organisms respond, recover, and continue to shape the ecosystems we depend on. 📅 Date: Monday, November 17 🕕 Time: 6:00 PM Come uncover the fascinating fight for life happening right under your feet!
events.descriptionHtml:
🌍 Living World Presents: The Afterlife of Roundup — Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health 🌱

The world beneath our feet is alive — teeming with hidden communities and remarkable resilience. In our next Living World event, Dr. Sharon Pochron will reveal how even the soil itself tells a story of survival and adaptation.

Join us as Dr. Pochron shares her research on the effects of Roundup and explores what happens beneath the surface in “The Afterlife of Roundup: Soil, Earthworms, and Plant Health.” Discover how soil organisms respond, recover, and continue to shape the ecosystems we depend on.

📅 Date: Monday, November 17
🕕 Time: 6:00 PM

Come uncover the fascinating fight for life happening right under your feet!

events.name: Ecology and Evolutions Society Club: Integrative Taxonomy of Calcareous Sponges
events.descriptionPlain: Sponges are thought to be one of the most peculiar animals on this planet. Calcareous sponges are the most obscure and unique sponges in a already unique species group. Raisa Rizzieri has dedicated much of her time studying and learning about these peculiar creatures and now she's ready to share her intriguing discoveries regarding the taxonomy of Calcareous Sponges. She will also be sharing valuable career advise for those looking to gain a research career or seeking higher education in Ecology and Evolution. Date: November 18th | 4:30PM - 5:30PM Location: Life Sciences | Conference Rm 650 There will be Free Food , Fun Games, and lots of Valuable Advise!

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: PASA's Christmas Party
events.descriptionPlain: Join the Polish American Student Association for our annual Christmas Party and celebrate the holidays with delicious traditional Polish food and festive cheer! Enjoy a cozy evening filled with good company, music, and authentic dishes, including: 🥟 Pierogi with sauerkraut 🥟 Pierogi with cheese and potato 🥟 Pierogi with meat 🌭 Kielbasa (Polish sausage) 🥒 Jar of pickles 🍞 Loaf of bread 🥤 Tymbark and orange soda Come hungry, bring your holiday spirit, and experience the warmth of a true Polish Christmas! Everyone is welcome — don’t miss out on one of PASA’s favorite end-of-year traditions!
events.descriptionHtml:

events.name: The Human Library
events.descriptionPlain: The Human Library at Stony Brook University is an in-person event designed to challenge stereotypes and prejudice through open dialogue with real people. We will have a number of Open Books available for attendees to "check out" for 30 minute conversations. Each book is a person with a story to tell based on their diverse backgrounds. Sponsored by University Libraries and the DEIA Team with the Division of Student Affairs, Office of Diversity, Inclusion and Intercultural Initiatives (DI3), Office of Military and Veteran Affairs, Program in Public Health and Diversity, Intercultural, and Community Engagement (DICE). We encourage RSVPs, but please note this event is first-come, first-served.

events.name: 11/19 Wicked 2: For Good: Pre- Screening!
events.descriptionPlain: ✨ Join Us for a Wickedly Wonderful Night! ✨ Join us on Wednesday, November 19th for a magical trip to Island 16 Cinema de Lux for an exclusive pre-screening of 🌟 Wicked: The Movie Part 2 🌟 see it before it officially hits theaters! 🎟️ Your Ticket Includes: 🏰 Round-trip travel to the theater 🎬 A private showing of Wicked: The Movie 🍫 Snacks to keep you fueled for the adventure 🍿 Special Treats: The first 50 students to purchase tickets will receive a FREE Medium Popcorn & Soft Drink Combo Voucher! After that, vouchers are available on a first-come, first-served basis — so grab your ticket before they fly away on a broomstick! ✨ 🚌 Travel Details: 🕕 The bus (our enchanted carriage for the evening 🏰) departs from the Stony Brook Union at 6:00 PM 🎥 The movie runs approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes 🚌 The bus will return to campus right after the credits roll 🚍 Transportation Policy: All students must use the provided transportation to and from the theater unless otherwise approved by the trip chaperone on the day of the event. ⚠️ Failure to follow this policy may result in suspension from future off-campus trips. 💰 Ticket Prices: 🎟 Undergraduate students: $10 🎟 Graduate students: $15 ✨✨ 🎫 TICKETS GO ON SALE MONDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH AT 9:00 AM! DON’T MISS YOUR CHANCE TO SNAG A SPOT BEFORE THEY FLY AWAY ON A BROOMSTICK! 🧹 ✨✨ CLICK HERE TO BUY TICKETS 🎟️ Tickets are non-transferable and non-refundable . 📧 For questions (or if you’re feeling a little green with excitement 💚), email BeyondtheBrook@stonybrook.edu .
events.descriptionHtml:
✨ Join Us for a Wickedly Wonderful Night! ✨

Join us on Wednesday, November 19th for a magical trip to Island 16 Cinema de Lux for an exclusive pre-screening of 🌟 Wicked: The Movie Part 2 🌟 

see it before it officially hits theaters!

🎟️ Your Ticket Includes:
🏰 Round-trip travel to the theater
🎬 A private showing of Wicked: The Movie
🍫 Snacks to keep you fueled for the adventure

🍿 Special Treats:
The first 50 students to purchase tickets will receive a FREE Medium Popcorn & Soft Drink Combo Voucher!
After that, vouchers are available on a first-come, first-served basis — so grab your ticket before they fly away on a broomstick! ✨

🚌 Travel Details:
🕕 The bus (our enchanted carriage for the evening 🏰) departs from the Stony Brook Union at 6:00 PM
🎥 The movie runs approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes
🚌 The bus will return to campus right after the credits roll

🚍 Transportation Policy:
All students must use the provided transportation to and from the theater unless otherwise approved by the trip chaperone on the day of the event.

⚠️ Failure to follow this policy may result in suspension from future off-campus trips.

💰 Ticket Prices:
🎟 Undergraduate students: $10
🎟 Graduate students: $15

✨✨
🎫 TICKETS GO ON SALE MONDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH AT 9:00 AM!
DON’T MISS YOUR CHANCE TO SNAG A SPOT BEFORE THEY FLY AWAY ON A BROOMSTICK! 🧹
✨✨

CLICK HERE TO BUY TICKETS

🎟️ Tickets are non-transferable and non-refundable.

📧 For questions (or if you’re feeling a little green with excitement 💚), email BeyondtheBrook@stonybrook.edu.

events.descriptionPlain: Have you been growing tired of staring at your phone, waiting for your one friend to text back? Yeah...we thought so...😬Why not go back to an old-school style of communication! Come join us for this event where we will provide you with all the necessary materials to make your own postcard to send to your friends or a brand new pen-pal! Let's craft a postcard that surely won't get you left on "read". Snacks and drinks will also be available, and all materials are provided on a first come, first served basis💌📬

events.descriptionPlain: Ever wonder what it’s like to build a career in science? Curious about the paths researchers and professors take and the challenges and breakthroughs along the way? events.name: Matcha Cafe
events.descriptionPlain: Take a break from the mid-semester stress and join us for a cozy evening at the Match Café ! We’ll be serving authentic matcha —a smooth, earthy green tea known for its calming yet energizing properties. Matcha contains L-theanine , an amino acid that promotes relaxation without drowsiness, helping to ease anxiety and improve focus—perfect for students needing a mindful reset. Enjoy the serene atmosphere with lo-fi beats playing in the background, and settle in to watch a chill movie while sipping your handcrafted matcha latte. Whether you’re here to destress, study in peace, or just vibe with friends, this café-style event is your chance to slow down and recharge. Details: 📍 Location: Hamilton Classroom 🕖 Time: November 19th, 2025 7:00-9:00 🎶 Featuring: Lo-fi music, authentic matcha, and a relaxing film 👥 Capacity: As supplies last Come for the tea, stay for the peace.
events.descriptionHtml:
Take a break from the mid-semester stress and join us for a cozy evening at the Match Café! We’ll be serving authentic matcha—a smooth, earthy green tea known for its calming yet energizing properties. Matcha contains L-theanine, an amino acid that promotes relaxation without drowsiness, helping to ease anxiety and improve focus—perfect for students needing a mindful reset.

Enjoy the serene atmosphere with lo-fi beats playing in the background, and settle in to watch a chill movie while sipping your handcrafted matcha latte. Whether you’re here to destress, study in peace, or just vibe with friends, this café-style event is your chance to slow down and recharge.

Details:
📍 Location: Hamilton Classroom
🕖 Time: November 19th, 2025 7:00-9:00
🎶 Featuring: Lo-fi music, authentic matcha, and a relaxing film
👥 Capacity: As supplies last

Come for the tea, stay for the peace.

events.name: Native American Heritage Month Movie Night: Apocalypto
events.descriptionPlain: Join us in honoring Native American Heritage Month with a special screening of Apocalypto (2006), directed by Mel Gibson. This visually powerful film follows the journey of a young man’s courage and resilience as he strives to protect his family and community amid the collapse of the Maya civilization. This event aims to spark meaningful conversation about Indigenous histories, storytelling, and representation in film. A brief discussion will follow the screening for those who wish to share reflections and insights. Date: November 21, 2025 Time: 6:00PM- 8:30PM Location: West MPR (Multi-Purpose Room) Light refreshments and snacks will be provided. If you require accessibility accommodations or have specific needs, please contact us at quenton.caine-jeffers@stonybrook.edu. We are happy to ensure everyone can fully participate and enjoy the event.

events.name: 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast
events.descriptionPlain: Rainbow Fall Feast is our annual celebration of our LGBTQ* communities at Stony Brook University and of the families that have been found, chosen, and built here. It serves as an opportunity for our students, faculty, staff, and alumni to come together and show thanks for the bonds we've formed during our time here. This year, we will be expanding our efforts to support the Stony Brook University Seawolves Pantry by collecting donations for the Food Pantry. Any and all non-expired, non-frozen food donations are welcome, but please consider donating one of the high-demand items listed on the Seawolves Pantry website . Donations will be accepted at event check-in, but can also be dropped off at the LGBTQ* Center or the UNITI Cultural Center during hours of operation, beginning Monday, November 10th. For every item you donate, you will receive 1 raffle ticket to enter to win one of several prize baskets, donated by our generous campus partners. You are not required to bring a donation to attend the event, but every donation is greatly appreciated. This year marks our 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast! Come join us for a night of great food, a DJ, games, crafts, dancing, and more! REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED! Seats fill fast, so please reserve your seat here: 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast Registration Form We look forward to seeing you! If you have any accessibility needs or concerns, please contact LGBTQ* Services at lgbtqservices@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-1690.
events.descriptionHtml:
Rainbow Fall Feast is our annual celebration of our LGBTQ* communities at Stony Brook University and of the families that have been found, chosen, and built here. It serves as an opportunity for our students, faculty, staff, and alumni to come together and show thanks for the bonds we've formed during our time here. 

 

This year, we will be expanding our efforts to support the Stony Brook University Seawolves Pantry by collecting donations for the Food Pantry. Any and all non-expired, non-frozen food donations are welcome, but please consider donating one of the high-demand items listed on the Seawolves Pantry website.

 

Donations will be accepted at event check-in, but can also be dropped off at the LGBTQ* Center or the UNITI Cultural Center during hours of operation, beginning Monday, November 10th.

 

For every item you donate, you will receive 1 raffle ticket to enter to win one of several prize baskets, donated by our generous campus partners. You are not required to bring a donation to attend the event, but every donation is greatly appreciated.

 

This year marks our 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast! Come join us for a night of great food, a DJ, games, crafts, dancing, and more! 

 

REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED! Seats fill fast, so please reserve your seat here: 13th Annual Rainbow Fall Feast Registration Form

 

We look forward to seeing you! If you have any accessibility needs or concerns, please contact LGBTQ* Services at lgbtqservices@stonybrook.edu or 631-632-1690.

events.descriptionPlain: ✨ Join Us for a Wickedly Wonderful Day Out! ✨ Join us on Sunday, November 23rd for a magical trip to Island 16 Cinema de Lux to see a regular showing of 🌟 Wicked 2: The Movie Part 2 🌟 on the big screen! 🎟️ Your Ticket Includes: 🏰 Round-trip travel to the theater 🎬 Admission to a regular showing of Wicked 2: The Movie 🍫 Snacks to keep you fueled for the adventure 🍿 Special Treats: The first 50 students to purchase tickets will receive a FREE Medium Popcorn & Soft Drink Combo Voucher! After that, vouchers will be available on a first-come, first-served basis — so act fast before they’re gone like magic! ✨ 🚌 Travel Details: 🕚 The bus (our enchanted carriage for the day 🏰) departs from the Stony Brook Union at 11:00 AM 🎥 Showtime begins at 12:00 PM ⏳ The movie runs about 2 hours and 40 minutes 🚌 The bus will return to campus right after the credits roll 🚍 Transportation Policy: All students must use the provided transportation to and from the theater unless otherwise approved by the trip chaperone on the day of the event. ⚠️ Failure to follow this policy may result in suspension from future off-campus trips. 💰 Ticket Prices: 🎟 Undergraduate students: $10 🎟 Graduate students: $15 ✨✨ 🎫 TICKETS GO ON SALE MONDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH AT 9:00 AM! DON’T MISS YOUR CHANCE TO SNAG A SPOT BEFORE THEY FLY AWAY ON A BROOMSTICK! 🧹 ✨✨ CLICK HERE TO BUY TICKETS 🎟️ Tickets are non-transferable and non-refundable . 📧 For questions (or if you’re feeling a little green with excitement 💚), email BeyondtheBrook@stonybrook.edu .
events.descriptionHtml:
✨ Join Us for a Wickedly Wonderful Day Out! ✨

Join us on Sunday, November 23rd for a magical trip to Island 16 Cinema de Lux to see a regular showing of 🌟 Wicked 2: The Movie Part 2 🌟 on the big screen!

🎟️ Your Ticket Includes:
🏰 Round-trip travel to the theater
🎬 Admission to a regular showing of Wicked 2: The Movie
🍫 Snacks to keep you fueled for the adventure

🍿 Special Treats:
The first 50 students to purchase tickets will receive a FREE Medium Popcorn & Soft Drink Combo Voucher!
After that, vouchers will be available on a first-come, first-served basis — so act fast before they’re gone like magic! ✨

🚌 Travel Details:
🕚 The bus (our enchanted carriage for the day 🏰) departs from the Stony Brook Union at 11:00 AM
🎥 Showtime begins at 12:00 PM
⏳ The movie runs about 2 hours and 40 minutes
🚌 The bus will return to campus right after the credits roll

🚍 Transportation Policy:
All students must use the provided transportation to and from the theater unless otherwise approved by the trip chaperone on the day of the event.
⚠️ Failure to follow this policy may result in suspension from future off-campus trips.

💰 Ticket Prices:
🎟 Undergraduate students: $10
🎟 Graduate students: $15

✨✨
🎫 TICKETS GO ON SALE MONDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH AT 9:00 AM!
DON’T MISS YOUR CHANCE TO SNAG A SPOT BEFORE THEY FLY AWAY ON A BROOMSTICK! 🧹
✨✨

CLICK HERE TO BUY TICKETS

🎟️ Tickets are non-transferable and non-refundable.

📧 For questions (or if you’re feeling a little green with excitement 💚), email BeyondtheBrook@stonybrook.edu.

events.name: GradConnect Series
events.descriptionPlain: We are excited to invite graduate students to the November GradConnect Series , organized by the Graduate School. Th is new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community. For the November GradConnect Series, event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students. Center for Prevention and Outreach (CPO) Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) Light refreshments will be provided. Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December! Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event .
events.descriptionHtml:
We are excited to invite graduate students to the November GradConnect Series, organized by the Graduate School.

 

This new monthly series will partner with on-campus and off-campus resources to provide students with direct opportunities to learn about available services and connect. The event will also be a platform for graduate students to interact with each other, share experiences, and build a supportive community.

 

For the  November GradConnect Series,  event, we are delighted that the following campus departments and resources are joining to support and connect with graduate students.

  • Center for Prevention and Outreach (CPO)
  • Counseling and Psychological Services (CAPS) 

 

Light refreshments will be provided.

 

 Plus, attend at least three of the four Fall 2025 events in the GradConnect Series to be entered into our Grand Raffle Prize drawing in December!  

 

Looking forward to seeing you at the GradConnect Series event.

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.
events.descriptionHtml:
Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation.

Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: Rohan Pradhan Guest Speech by Amazon
events.descriptionPlain: Amazon: Observations on Reasoning and the Gullibility of Language Models 📆 Date: Monday, December 1 🕓 Time: 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM 📍 Location: SAC 305 🗣 Speaker: Rohan Pradhan, working on applied research in Generative AI at Amazon. Students will have the opportunity to hear from Rohan Pradhan, Applied Scientist at Amazon and a leading voice in Generative AI and Frontier Models research. The event will feature: • 🎤 Talk: A presentation by Rohan Pradhan on the reliability of AI systems • ❓ Q&A: An open forum for student questions • 🤝 Networking: A chance to personally speak with Rohan Pradhan after the session • 🍕 Food: Pizza will be served! All students are welcome—no prior AI experience required.
events.descriptionHtml:
Amazon: Observations on Reasoning and the Gullibility of Language Models

📆 Date: Monday, December 1

🕓 Time: 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM

📍 Location: SAC 305

🗣 Speaker: Rohan Pradhan, working on applied research in Generative AI at Amazon.

Students will have the opportunity to hear from Rohan Pradhan, Applied Scientist at Amazon and a leading voice in Generative AI and Frontier Models research.

The event will feature:

• 🎤 Talk: A presentation by Rohan Pradhan on the reliability of AI systems

• ❓ Q&A: An open forum for student questions

events.name: Russian Corner and Hillel New Year’s Celebration
events.descriptionPlain: Join Russian Corner and Hillel for Noviy God, the traditional Russian New Year celebration, on December 2 at 7 PM in the SAC Shore Club! Step into the magic of a Russian winter evening filled with delicious food, festive traditions, and joyful celebration. Enjoy classic New Year’s dishes, lively music, and games that capture the warmth and spirit of this beloved holiday. Experience Russian New Year customs — from Ded Moroz (Grandfather Frost) and Snegurochka (the Snow Maiden)to the sparkle of the New Year tree and toasts for good fortune in the coming year. Whether you’re familiar with Russian culture or just looking for a fun and welcoming celebration, everyone is invited to join the festivities!

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
