events.name: GSO END-OF-SEMESTER PARTY - 2025
events.descriptionPlain: This semester’s been a grind for everyone, and this is that one evening where you just show up, unwind, and enjoy the vibe. The GSO End-of-Semester Party on 3rd December 2025 is going to have everything you’d hope for - good food, live performances, music, and a full DJ set to close out the night. We’re keeping the atmosphere fun and relaxed. If you want to come, eat, chill with friends, take photos, dance a little, or just sit back and enjoy the performances, you’re welcome. If you want to perform, even better - we love seeing students bring their talent on stage. We’ll be putting together a short lineup of performers, so we’re opening an Interest Form for anyone who wants a slot. Here’s what you should know before signing up: Filling the form does not guarantee a performance slot. Slots will be decided on a first-come, first-serve basis . The final lineup depends on the available time and flow of the event . If you're selected, we’ll reach out with confirmation and details. If you’re interested in performing (music, dance, stand-up, poetry - anything you’d like to showcase), fill out the form here: 👉 Performance Interest Form: https://forms.gle/fdCNjcKP5Zvn9B3N8 Come for the food. Come for the music. Come for the vibe. See you on December 3rd at SAC Ballroom A , 6 PM to 8 PM.
events.descriptionHtml:
This semester’s been a grind for everyone, and this is that one evening where you just show up, unwind, and enjoy the vibe. The GSO End-of-Semester Party on 3rd December 2025 is going to have everything you’d hope for - good food, live performances, music, and a full DJ set to close out the night.

We’re keeping the atmosphere fun and relaxed. If you want to come, eat, chill with friends, take photos, dance a little, or just sit back and enjoy the performances, you’re welcome. If you want to perform, even better - we love seeing students bring their talent on stage.

We’ll be putting together a short lineup of performers, so we’re opening an Interest Form for anyone who wants a slot. Here’s what you should know before signing up:

  • Filling the form does not guarantee a performance slot.

  • Slots will be decided on a first-come, first-serve basis.

  • The final lineup depends on the available time and flow of the event.

  • If you're selected, we’ll reach out with confirmation and details.

If you’re interested in performing (music, dance, stand-up, poetry - anything you’d like to showcase), fill out the form here:

👉 Performance Interest Form: https://forms.gle/fdCNjcKP5Zvn9B3N8

 

Come for the food. Come for the music. Come for the vibe.


See you on December 3rd at SAC Ballroom A, 6 PM to 8 PM.

events.name: HOSA Mock-Conference RSVP
events.descriptionPlain: HOSA is hosting a Mock-Conference to give you a low-pressure taste of real competition season before the Spring Leadership Conference! 🎓🩺 Join us on Wednesday, December 4th from 11:30 AM–1:30 PM in SAC 303 for HOSA-style competitive events, including HOSA Bowl, Medical/Dental Terminology, Biomedical Debate, and a Spelling Bee . You’ll get to experience how events are run, practice presenting and thinking on your feet, and build confidence for future conferences, interviews, and professional programs. The event is open to all levels —from complete beginners to experienced competitors—and we encourage business casual attire if possible. Come to test your knowledge, connect with other members and the E-board, and enjoy free food . And yes, you can bring friends , even if they don’t plan to compete in the actual conferences!

HOSA is hosting a Mock-Conference to give you a low-pressure taste of real competition season before the Spring Leadership Conference! 🎓🩺

The event is open to all levels—from complete beginners to experienced competitors—and we encourage business casual attire if possible. events.name: Serverless & Supabase:Hackathon Workshop
events.descriptionPlain: Want to build full-stack apps with no backend code? Join the Web Development Club for our hands-on workshop: Build a Serverless App with Supabase In this workshop, you'll learn how to: Set up a Supabase project with authentication, database, and APIs Build a complete web app using only frontend code Add real-time functionality using Supabase's built-in tools Deploy and share your project quickly — perfect for hackathons Tech Stack: Database & Auth: Supabase (open-source Firebase alternative) Frontend: React, Vite, Tailwind CSS Dev Tools: Supabase Studio, GitHub Codespaces (no local setup needed) Spin up a browser-based environment and follow along as we build a serverless app from scratch in under an hour. If you're interested in fast, modern web development without the backend complexity — this workshop is for you. Come code with us!
events.descriptionHtml:
Want to build full-stack apps with no backend code?

Join the Web Development Club for our hands-on workshop: Build a Serverless App with Supabase

In this workshop, you'll learn how to:

  • Set up a Supabase project with authentication, database, and APIs

  • Build a complete web app using only frontend code

  • Add real-time functionality using Supabase's built-in tools

  • Deploy and share your project quickly — perfect for hackathons

Tech Stack:

  • Database & Auth: Supabase (open-source Firebase alternative)

  • Frontend: React, Vite, Tailwind CSS

  • Dev Tools: Supabase Studio, GitHub Codespaces (no local setup needed)

Spin up a browser-based environment and follow along as we build a serverless app from scratch in under an hour.

If you're interested in fast, modern web development without the backend complexity — this workshop is for you.

Come code with us!

events.descriptionPlain: Get ready for a night full of music, culture, and movement! Noche de Baile is a celebration of Latin rhythm and community, brought to you by Jubilé Latino . Join us as we fill the floor with the vibrant sounds of salsa, bachata, merengue, reggaetón, and more! Whether you’re a beginner learning your first steps or a seasoned dancer ready to show off your style, this event is all about joy, connection, and embracing Latinx culture through dance. Come with friends, meet new people, and enjoy an unforgettable night in a space filled with energy and positivity. Let’s light up the night together. ¡Nos vemos en la pista! ✨

events.name: Finals Fuel-Up
events.descriptionPlain: Take a break from the end-of-semester chaos and recharge with your community! Join us for a cozy board game night where you can unwind, de-stress, and enjoy some friendly competition. While you’re here, grab a Finals Study Survival Kit —created in partnership with New Student & Transition Programs—packed with snacks, tea, study tools, and other essentials to help you power through finals. This event is especially helpful for first-year students who are experiencing their first finals week. It’s a chance to connect with peers, learn healthy study habits, ask questions, and pick up resources that make the transition into college academics feel smoother and more manageable. Come relax, refuel, and get ready to finish the semester strong!

events.name: Game Night at Benedict
events.descriptionPlain: Get ready for the ultimate chill night at Benedict! Join the Benedict Hall Council for a fun and competitive Game Night filled with poker, bingo, Uno, and more. Whether you want to play like a pro or just hang out with friends, this is the perfect way to unwind, meet new people, and enjoy good vibes. We’ll have light background music , free snacks , and soft drinks (first come, first serve), so come early. Feeling competitive? Win rounds in our games to earn raffle tickets . The person with the most raffles at the end of the night will walk away with an OWALA Water Bottle – yes, the hype is real. Everyone is welcome. Come to play, come to compete, or just come to vibe. Bring your friends. Bring your energy. Bring your game face. 📅 4 December 2025 📍 Benedict South Lounge ⏰ 8 PM – 9 PM Hope to see you all there!
events.descriptionHtml:
Get ready for the ultimate chill night at Benedict!
Join the Benedict Hall Council for a fun and competitive Game Night filled with poker, bingo, Uno, and more. Whether you want to play like a pro or just hang out with friends, this is the perfect way to unwind, meet new people, and enjoy good vibes.

 

We’ll have light background music, free snacks, and soft drinks (first come, first serve), so come early.

 

Feeling competitive?
Win rounds in our games to earn raffle tickets. The person with the most raffles at the end of the night will walk away with an OWALA Water Bottle – yes, the hype is real.

 

Everyone is welcome. Come to play, come to compete, or just come to vibe.
Bring your friends. Bring your energy. Bring your game face.

 

📅 4 December 2025
📍 Benedict South Lounge

⏰ 8 PM – 9 PM

Hope to see you all there!

events.name: Chinese Food Shabbat
events.descriptionPlain: Chinese Food Shabbat: The Ultimate Friday Night Mashup 🍜✨ Forget everything you thought you knew about Shabbat dinner… because this week, we’re serving up Chinese food, Hillel-style ! Think the comfort of Shabbat vibes + the joy of scallion pancakes, lo mein, dumplings, and all your takeout favorites — all in one delicious, communal, vibey night. Join us for a laid-back, laughter-filled evening with great food, great friends, and zero prep dishes. Whether you’re craving sesame noodles or just looking for a warm space to unwind after a long week, this is the perfect spot. Come hungry. Leave happy. Bring your friends. Shabbat shalom… but make it delicious 😌🥡

events.name: FCC Grow a Rice Paddy
events.descriptionPlain: Welcome to Grow a Rice Paddy! Learn about the origins of rice and its role in Fujianese history, with a theme similar to the popular Roblox game, Grow a Garden. You can explore interactive games, enjoy delicious food, and participate in engaging activities that highlight rice. The event also features a selection of food and drinks that reflect authentic flavors. Come to Grow a Rice Paddy and have a great time with the Fujianese Culture Club!

events.name: Wolfie's Big Band Pizza and Jazz
events.descriptionPlain: Feeling that chill in the air, or just want to chill before finals? Step into a Winter Wonderland at Wolfie’s Big Band Pizza and Jazz! Get entranced in the mystical, snowy vibes and cozy up with hot cocoa piled high with marshmallows , frosted cookies , and fresh, cheesy pizza while the Jazz Band fills the night with cool, smooth tunes. Grab a free candy cane , chill out while dressed up , and let the music melt away your worries. It’s the perfect way to relax, recharge, and soak in those magical winter vibes before finals week begins! This is also a fundraiser for Brain2Brain , where each donation goes to support people facing Glioblastoma .

events.name: Cocoa & Cramming
events.descriptionPlain: Take a moment to slow down and breathe before finals season hits. Cocoa & Cramming is a cozy wellness space designed to help you unwind and reset. Residents can enjoy calming hot chocolate, light snacks, and a peaceful atmosphere with soft music and low lights. We’ll have simple mindfulness activities—including guided breathing cards, reflection prompts, and a small gratitude board—to help you release stress and feel grounded. Whether you stay for five minutes or the whole time, this night is all about taking care of your mind and giving yourself a well-deserved break.

events.name: Voices y Vibras: Culture, Careers & Community
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for an evening that celebrates culture, identity, and professional growth. The event opens with a Latiné Professional Panel, where speakers from fields like health, business, and education will share their career journeys and how their cultural backgrounds shaped their experiences. After the panel, residents will take part in the We Are Yang Banner Activity by adding designs, colors, or symbols that represent their heritage. The completed banner will be displayed in Yang Hall as a showcase of our community’s diversity. Empanadas (from Chico’s Tex Mex) will be provided.
events.descriptionHtml:
Join us for an evening that celebrates culture, identity, and professional growth. The event opens with a Latiné Professional Panel, where speakers from fields like health, business, and education will share their career journeys and how their cultural backgrounds shaped their experiences.

After the panel, residents will take part in the We Are Yang Banner Activity by adding designs, colors, or symbols that represent their heritage. The completed banner will be displayed in Yang Hall as a showcase of our community’s diversity.

Empanadas (from Chico’s Tex Mex) will be provided.

events.name: Studyathon
events.descriptionPlain: 📚 Ready to crush your study session on Reading Day? Join RAs Maxime, Justin, Cibu, and Jouzef at The Studyathon for a power-packed study event that’ll keep you fueled and focused! 📅 When: Tuesday, December 9th, 12:30 PM - 2:30 PM 📍 Where: West E Commons, MPR 🍔 What's on the Menu: Buffalo Wild Wings + Celsius Energy Drinks (because nothing fuels a study grind like wings and energy drinks!) Lock in with us: whether you’re tackling assignments, reviewing for finals, or just need a quiet place to study. Come for the food, stay for the focus! Feel free to stick around after 2:30 PM if you want to keep studying, this is your space to stay as long as you need! ✨ Why Attend? Free Buffalo Wild Wings and Celsius Energy Drinks to keep you energized! A relaxed, study-friendly space to help you prep for finals week. Perfect spot to meet up with friends or make some new ones while studying! Come grab some wings, sip on a Celsius, and get ready to ace your finals!

events.name: SBU Comp Prog Weekly Meeting
events.descriptionPlain: Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams. The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!
events.descriptionHtml:
Every week, we meet on Tuesday at 7pm in the Old CS Building Room 2114. At each meeting, we hold a practice contest on vjudge.com, and we have free pizza. 

Stony Brook Competitive Programming Club is a place for anyone interested in algorithms and programming. We offer lectures on algorithmic problems solving, host speakers to discuss algorithms, and provide training sessions for our ICPC teams.
The weekly contest takes place on vjudge.com, where participants are presented with a set of problems to solve within specified time and memory constraints. Solutions can be submitted in various programming languages, including C, C++, Java, and Python, among others. All students from SBU are encouraged to participate. We usually have a collaborative environment where everyone can seek assistance from each other. If you are new to competitive programming and need some guidance, don't hesitate to reach out to someone there for help!

events.name: Sunrise GBM
events.descriptionPlain: Sunrise SBU GBMs are a weekly space where student climate activists, organizers, and new members come together to connect, plan, and build collective power to fight for climate justice at Stony Brook University, in New York State, and across the nation. Sunrise SBU is a hub of the Sunrise Movement, a youth-led movement to stop climate change and create millions of good jobs through a Green New Deal. As a hub, we work to empower students to take action through grassroots organizing, political education, and coalition-building.

events.name: Art and Mindfulness
events.descriptionPlain: Join us for a relaxing session that blends art and meditation. This practice offers a space to unwind, reflect, and explore the present moment through sacred patterns and guided drawing activities, all while enjoying warm tea and light tea biscuits. Activities include: Printed Mandalas & Yantras Coloring – Relax as you color traditional sacred designs using colored pencils, markers, or crayons. Guided Mandala Drawing – Create your own mandala for centering and self-reflection with pens, markers, or colored pencils. Opposite-Hand & Eyes-Closed Drawing Challenges – Playful exercises to release perfectionism, spark intuition, and deepen mindfulness using the same art materials on paper. No art experience needed, all skill levels are welcome! Materials, tea, and tea biscuits will be provided.
events.descriptionHtml:
Join us for a relaxing session that blends art and meditation. This practice offers a space to unwind, reflect, and explore the present moment through sacred patterns and guided drawing activities, all while enjoying warm tea and light tea biscuits.

Activities include:

Printed Mandalas & Yantras Coloring – Relax as you color traditional sacred designs using colored pencils, markers, or crayons.

Guided Mandala Drawing – Create your own mandala for centering and self-reflection with pens, markers, or colored pencils.

Opposite-Hand & Eyes-Closed Drawing Challenges – Playful exercises to release perfectionism, spark intuition, and deepen mindfulness using the same art materials on paper.

No art experience needed, all skill levels are welcome!


Materials, tea, and tea biscuits will be provided.

events.name: Yalda Night
events.descriptionPlain: Yalda Night (Shab-e Yalda) is one of the oldest and most cherished Iranian celebrations, marking the longest night of the year — the winter solstice. Rooted in ancient Persian traditions and Zoroastrian beliefs, it symbolizes the victory of light over darkness as days gradually begin to lengthen after the solstice. We will gather to celebrate this night with some symbolic fruits such as watermelon (for health and longevity), pomegranates (representing rebirth and the cycle of life), and nuts and of coure persian food . These foods symbolize vitality, protection from illness, and the promise of light’s return. And the works of the Persian poet Hafez are especially recited.
events.descriptionHtml:

These foods symbolize vitality, protection from illness, and the promise of light’s return. And the works of the Persian poet Hafez are especially recited.
